This makes a 35-window scaffold mechanically plausible: repeat the same controlled window with branch controls from the low-ratio generator/history.
asi.caveat: The sample window uses classical preset controls. A production DIV still needs reversible control generation/decoding, cleanup of controls/history, canonical controlled neg, and scratch reduction of the controlled modular add workspace.
asi.next_action_hint: Build a cost-only 35-window/560-step scaffold with allocated controls to confirm total ops/peak, then replace preset controls with branch-pattern history decoding. Separately implement canonical controlled neg or prove zero exceptions negligible for tagged channel.

description: Instantiate full 560-step scaled BY arithmetic scaffold

asi.hypothesis: A full 560-step scaled BY selected-replay scaffold with raw controls should fit the current 2800q cap and confirm the one-step 1.145M arithmetic extrapolation as an actual circuit skeleton.
asi.result: Added scaled_by_controlled_560_scaffold_cost_model_fits_current_cap. It allocates raw odd/A controls for all 560 steps and instantiates the scaled controlled microstep repeatedly. Cost: 1,145,760 CCX, peak=2,405q, raw_control_bits=1120. Full BY tests pass locally (58 passed). ./autoresearch.sh default path unchanged and passes at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The full selected BY arithmetic scaffold is now concrete and under the current qubit cap even with raw controls. This is a major step toward an env-gated BY tagged-DIV prototype: arithmetic no longer blocks either Toffoli or the 2800q cap. The remaining gap to the user's ~600-scratch goal is history compression/workspace aliasing, not the core replay circuit.
asi.caveat: Raw controls are advice qubits, not generated/cleaned history. Peak 2405q is under current cap but far above a strict tx,ty+600 target. Need branch-pattern decoding and no-clean-temp/dirty controlled add to avoid history+adder-temp addition.
asi.next_action_hint: Next connect controls to branch-pattern history: decode one 16-bit window pattern into odd/A controls, run the 16-step window, then uncompute the pattern. In parallel, design a canonical controlled neg to remove the zero->p caveat.

description: Show BY A-controls decode from 16-bit branch patterns plus delta

asi.hypothesis: For the scaled BY microprogram, raw odd/A controls double the apparent history. A-controls should be reconstructible from the 16 odd bits in a window plus the starting delta, so only branch patterns need to be stored/compressed.
asi.result: Added window_pattern_and_delta_reconstruct_a_controls. For 20,000 sampled lowword windows, a 16-bit odd-pattern and starting delta reconstruct all 16 A-control bits and the final delta, matching direct lowword divstep evolution. Full BY tests pass locally (59 passed). ./autoresearch.sh default path unchanged and passes at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: A bits are decoder scratch, not persistent history. The compressed branch-pattern history (≈440 entropy / 481 fixed distinct-pattern bits) is the right payload for the 560-step scaled BY scaffold; per-window A controls can be regenerated locally from pattern+delta.
asi.caveat: This is classical reconstruction, not a reversible decoder circuit. Need synthesize a small delta/A-mask decoder and ensure it uncomputes cleanly after each 16-step controlled window.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement/cost a reversible 16-bit pattern decoder: pattern + delta -> odd controls + A controls + next delta, feed one controlled window, then uncompute. Keep decoder workspace under ~32 bits so compressed-history + no-clean controlled add can plausibly fit ~600 scratch.

description: Budget BY branch-pattern decoder inside integration margin

asi.hypothesis: The branch-pattern history decoder should be negligible compared with the 1.145M scaled BY modular replay and fit inside the 150k branch/decode margin in the point-add SOTA budget.
asi.result: Added pattern_decoder_budget_fits_branch_decode_margin. A pessimistic reversible estimate with 10-bit signed delta uses ~41 CCX per divstep (sign/nonzero predicate, A-bit write, controlled delta update), or ~22,960 CCX over 560 steps. Full BY tests pass locally (60 passed). ./autoresearch.sh default path unchanged and passes at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The branch-pattern decoder is not an arithmetic bottleneck. The 150k branch/decode margin in the 2.238M point-add projection is generous if the decoder is implemented directly. The main remaining scratch problem is controlled modular-add workspace and compressed-history storage, not A-bit reconstruction.
asi.caveat: This is a budget estimate, not a synthesized reversible decoder. It assumes 10-bit delta remains ample and ignores constant factors in a real comparator/nonzero tree, but has >6x margin under 150k.
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to either a reversible decoder prototype or the no-clean-temp/dirty controlled modular add. The latter is higher priority for the ~600 scratch target.

description: Exercise zero inputs in scaled BY controlled microstep

asi.hypothesis: The scaled controlled microstep uses fast controlled negation that temporarily maps zero to the noncanonical representative p. In valid A cases, the subsequent controlled add should canonicalize this representative, so zero inputs should still behave correctly.
asi.result: Extended scaled_by_controlled_microstep_matches_all_cases_and_hits_target_cost to include explicit zero cases (r,s)=(0,0),(0,s),(r,0) for all valid C/B/A controls, in addition to random basis states. The test still passes with unchanged cost 2046 CCX and peak 1287q. Full BY tests pass locally (60 passed). ./autoresearch.sh default path unchanged and passes at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The controlled-neg zero representative is not a blocker for valid scaled BY microstep controls: A always also has odd=1, so the following cmod_add canonicalizes the temporary p representative before the halve. This reduces one production caveat for the scaled BY scaffold.
asi.caveat: This only validates one microstep and valid controls. A full 560-step scaffold with generated controls still needs end-to-end tagged-DIV basis validation.
asi.next_action_hint: Build an end-to-end 35-window tagged-DIV basis simulation with controls derived from a sampled denominator, or an env-gated point-add probe once the interface is clear.

description: Simulate full 560-step scaled BY tagged-DIV basis circuit

asi.hypothesis: The full 560-step scaled controlled scaffold should not only have the right cost; with controls derived from a denominator it should perform the tagged-DIV transformation (0,y+x)->(r,0) and recover y/x as sign(f)*r-1.
asi.result: Added scaled_by_controlled_560_tagged_div_basis_simulation. For one sampled denominator that converges within 560 steps, the test derives all odd/A controls, instantiates and simulates the full 1,145,760-CCX circuit with raw controls, starts (r,s)=(0,y+x), verifies the simulated r,s match classical scaled BY, verifies s=0, and checks sign(f)*r-1 equals y/x. Peak is 2405q. Full BY tests pass locally (61 passed). ./autoresearch.sh default path unchanged and passes at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: This is the first end-to-end circuit-level tagged-DIV evidence for the scaled BY route. The arithmetic core is no longer just cost/model algebra; it simulates through all 560 controlled steps and recovers the quotient. The remaining work is replacing raw advice controls with reversible branch-pattern history/decoder and reducing scratch, then integrating into point_add.
asi.caveat: The simulation uses preset controls derived classically from x and only one sampled converged denominator. It does not yet generate/uncompute controls inside the circuit, and raw control qubits inflate peak to 2405q. Need broader samples or an env-gated scaffold with branch decoder.
asi.next_action_hint: Build a 35-window branch-pattern-history scaffold: store/decode patterns instead of raw odd/A controls, generate A controls from delta, run each 16-step window, and uncompute decoder scratch. Then integrate as KAL/BY tagged-DIV probe.

description: Quantify 600-scratch condition for compressed BY history

asi.hypothesis: The scaled BY DIV arithmetic and compressed branch-pattern history should fit the user's ~600 scratch target only if the controlled modular add does not allocate a clean 256-bit addend/carry workspace in addition to history.
asi.result: Added compressed_pattern_history_scratch_model_is_600q_if_add_workspace_is_removed. Current local microstep peak is 1287q for the two 256-bit data rows, i.e. 775 scratch. Compressed pattern history is 481q and one-window A/delta decoder scratch is ~26q, so current additive scratch≈1282q. If the controlled modular add is implemented without a clean n-bit temp or borrows the history bank as dirty workspace, using a 90q local target gives total scratch≈597q. Full BY tests pass locally (62 passed). ./autoresearch.sh default path unchanged and passes at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The exact remaining scratch condition is now clear. The branch-pattern history is small enough; the current clean-temp modular add is the reason the design is not yet tx,ty+600. A no-clean-temp/dirty controlled modular add is not optional for the user's scratch target, but if achieved the scaled BY route fits both Toffoli and scratch models.
asi.caveat: The 90q local workspace target is aspirational and requires a new controlled modular adder or dirty-history borrowing scheme. This test is a scratch accounting model, not a circuit implementation.
asi.next_action_hint: Design/prototype a controlled modular add that uses no clean n-bit addend register: e.g. controlled Cuccaro with one/few clean ancillae, or a dirty-ancilla version borrowing the compressed history bank and restoring it.

description: Replace raw BY controls with raw branch-pattern history scaffold

asi.hypothesis: A-controls are decoder scratch, not persistent history. Replacing 1120 raw odd/A controls by 560 raw odd-pattern bits plus one 16-bit A scratch window should preserve the tagged-DIV simulation and substantially reduce peak.
asi.result: Added scaled_by_pattern_history_560_tagged_div_scaffold_reduces_peak. For one sampled converged denominator, it allocates 560 raw pattern bits and a 16-bit per-window A scratch register, runs the same 560 scaled controlled microsteps, simulates (0,y+x), verifies bottom channel zero, and recovers y/x. Cost remains 1,145,760 CCX; peak drops from 2,405q with raw odd/A controls to 1,861q. Full BY tests pass locally (63 passed). ./autoresearch.sh default path unchanged and passes at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The branch-pattern history scaffold is concrete and works at circuit level with preset per-window A scratch. This narrows the implementation gap: the next decoder only needs to turn pattern+delta into one window's A scratch, not carry a full A history. Peak is now dominated by raw pattern bits plus current clean-temp cmod_add workspace.
asi.caveat: A scratch is loaded classically in this scaffold, not computed reversibly from pattern+delta. Pattern bits are still raw 560 bits, not entropy-compressed 481 bits. Current cmod_add clean temp still prevents the strict ~600-scratch target.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement reversible pattern+delta -> A-window decoder, then attack cmod_add workspace. If a no-clean/dirty controlled add lands, replace raw pattern with compressed pattern IDs.

description: Identify dirty qoffset adder substrate for 600-scratch BY

asi.hypothesis: The remaining 600-scratch blocker is no-clean-temp controlled modular add. The existing venting module may already provide a dirty quantum-offset adder with the right workspace/cost scale, which can be adapted into the missing controlled modular add substrate.
asi.result: Added dirty_quantum_offset_adder_is_plausible_cmod_add_substrate. Existing venting::iadd_dirty_2clean_qoffset adds a 256-bit quantum offset using 762 CCX, with exactly 2 clean qubits and 254 dirty qubits, no hidden clean n-register. Full BY tests pass locally (64 passed). ./autoresearch.sh default path unchanged and passes at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The desired adder family is present in the codebase: dirty quantum-offset addition already has SOTA-shaped workspace and cost. The next step is controlled dirty q-offset modular addition. If adapted, the compressed branch-pattern history bank can plausibly serve as dirty workspace and bring the scaled BY DIV primitive to the ~600-scratch target.
asi.caveat: The measured primitive is not controlled and not modular reduction by p. It is only the addition substrate. Controlled qoffset logic and modular reduction/canonicalization must be implemented and simulated before claiming the scratch target.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement a controlled variant of venting::iadd_dirty_2clean_qoffset or a wrapper suitable for cmod_add_qq: effective offset = ctrl ? q_offset : 0, using dirty history qubits and 2 clean ancillae. Then plug it into the scaled BY microstep cost/peak probe.

description: Prototype controlled dirty qoffset adder and invalidate naive control cost

asi.hypothesis: The remaining 600-scratch blocker is controlled modular addition without a clean 256-bit addend. A controlled dirty quantum-offset adder adapted from venting may preserve the scratch shape; measure whether naive control is also Toffoli-viable.
asi.result: Added ciadd_dirty_3clean_qoffset in venting.rs and tests in by.rs. Small n=8 basis check passes with target updated iff ctrl=1, offset preserved, dirty restored, and phase clean. n=256 naive controlled dirty qoffset costs 3557 CCX and peak 770q with 254 dirty + 3 clean; scratch shape is right, but scaled BY step would be ~4323 CCX and 560-step replay ~2.42M, too high. Full BY tests passed locally (66 passed). Latest autoresearch run was blocked by the context-window guard before benchmarking; default path was unchanged in prior runs at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: Dirty qoffset addition solves the clean-workspace/scratch direction, but naively controlling every qoffset interaction destroys the Toffoli win. The needed primitive is not just dirty and controlled; it must share the control efficiently, likely by fusing control into modular reduction or by computing a ctrl-masked offset once using dirty/history workspace without generic triple-control overhead.
asi.caveat: ciadd_dirty_3clean_qoffset is a prototype substrate, not a modular add by p and not integrated into scaled BY. The n=256 cost is an invalidation of naive control, not a production primitive.
asi.next_action_hint: Derive a control-efficient dirty cmod_add: options are pre-mask offset into dirty history once then use uncontrolled iadd_dirty_2clean_qoffset, controlled modular reduction with classical p using ciadd_dirty_2clean_classical, or a measurement/phase trick to avoid CCCX-per-bit control.

description: Account for tagged-DIV deleting pair1 multiplications in SOTA budget

asi.hypothesis: The whole-point scaled-BY DIV budget was conservative because it removed the two Kaliski invocations but kept pair1's two schoolbook multiplications. A true tagged DIV maps (0,dy+dx)->(lambda+1,0), so those multiplications should also disappear, giving enough Toffoli margin for the low-scratch/vented variant even if it is slower.
asi.result: Added low_scratch_scaled_by_budget_still_beats_27m_after_pair1_mul_deletion. It uses measured schoolbook costs 149,889 and 150,145 CCX. After deleting both Kaliski invocations and pair1's two multiplications, scaffold_after_div≈642,716. Fast scaled BY projection: ≈1,938,476 Toffoli. Low-scratch vented scaled BY projection (3318*560 plus 150k margin): ≈2,650,796 Toffoli, still under Google's 2.7M low-qubit target. Full BY tests passed locally (67 passed). Latest autoresearch run was blocked by context-window guard before benchmark; default path unchanged from prior passing runs at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The low-scratch path has more Toffoli margin than previously modeled. Even if the no-clean/vented modular-add route costs ~1.86M for the BY DIV core, deleting pair1's two multiplications keeps the whole point-add projection under 2.7M. This makes the 600-scratch engineering path viable without needing the fastest clean-temp adder.
asi.caveat: Still a budget model, not integrated point_add. It assumes tagged DIV can replace the pair1 Kaliski+mul cleanup and that the remaining scaffold remains roughly unchanged. Pair2 cleanup integration must be made explicit.
asi.next_action_hint: Next formalize the replacement schedule in point_add: identify exactly which current phases disappear (pair1 Kaliski, pair1_mul1, pair1_mul2, pair2 Kaliski) and what BY-DIV output registers feed into lam/tx/ty. Then build an env-gated scaffold or a circuit-level phase budget.

description: Prove neg-r inverse scaled-BY replay cleans pair2 product at forward cost

asi.hypothesis: The pair2 cleanup can be performed by running scaled BY backwards: forward maps (0,q*x)->(sign*q,0), so inverse maps (sign*q,0)->(0,q*x). If the inverse replay uses u=-r as the first channel, the inverse B case uses addition rather than subtraction and should match the forward 2046-CCX step cost.
asi.result: Added inverse_scaled_by_560_negr_frame_recovers_fast_cost. It simulates a full 560-step inverse product-clean replay with controls from a sampled converged denominator and input u=-sign*q, s=0. Output is logical zero in u and q*x in s. Cost is 1,145,760 CCX and peak 2,405q, matching the forward replay. BY suite passes locally: 69 tests passed.
asi.structural_learning: The full replacement is now two scaled-BY replays, not one replay plus an ordinary product cleanup: forward tagged DIV for pair1 to produce lambda+1, and neg-r inverse scaled BY for pair2 to clean lambda while writing q*tx=Ry+Qy. This deletes the two Kaliski bodies and the surrounding schoolbook product cleanup. The two-fast-replay budget with measured decoder margin is ≈2,637,286 Toffoli, below Google's 2.7M low-qubit target.
asi.caveat: This is still a circuit scaffold/budget model under test controls, not wired into build_standard_point_add. Logical zero can be the noncanonical representative p because of fast controlled negation; canonical unloading or a canonical neg variant remains to be synthesized.
asi.next_action_hint: Build the env-gated point-add phase schedule next: pair1 forward tagged-DIV on (0,dy+dx), sign/frame correction to lambda; tx=lambda^2-Px-Qx; pair2 neg-r inverse BY on (u=-q,0) with x=tx to write Ry+Qy and clean lambda; final subtract Qy. Then replace raw controls by recompute/decode history.

description: Wire scaled-BY replay scaffold into benchmark path behind env flag

asi.hypothesis: A safe way to test the scaled-BY thesis in the benchmark path immediately is to append a clean, env-gated no-op 560-step raw-pattern replay after the exact point-add output. This should exercise the real scaled-BY gate body, harness correctness, alternate-seed diagnostics, free-clean checks, and qubit analyzer without risking default correctness.
asi.result: Added BY_REPLAY_BENCH_SCAFFOLD=1 in src/point_add/mod.rs. It appends the actual 560-step scaled-BY microstep body using 560 persistent odd-pattern qubits plus a 16-qubit A scratch window and zero data/controls. Benchmark with the flag passed: avg_toffoli=5,278,510, emitted_ops=42,250,936, qubits=2,716, correctness_ok=1, altseed failures all zero. Default autoresearch path remains unchanged and passed: avg_toffoli=4,132,750, emitted_ops=31,193,208, qubits=2,716.
asi.structural_learning: The replay body can live inside the production point-add harness without increasing peak qubits beyond the current 2,716 because it reuses freed scratch after the exact output is computed. The measured delta from default is exactly the expected 1,145,760 Toffoli, validating that the benchmark path sees the same scaled-BY cost as the unit scaffolds.
asi.caveat: This is not yet the replacement path and does not beat SOTA by itself; it is a benchmark-path smoke hook. The next integration step must move the replay from no-op shadow registers onto tx/ty/lam and replace the Kaliski+mul phases instead of appending to them.
asi.next_action_hint: Use this hook to keep benchmark-path regressions visible while wiring the actual env-gated replacement: pair1 forward tagged DIV on (0,dy+dx), then pair2 neg-r inverse BY product-clean. Next expose real branch-pattern generation/decode or temporarily derive controls from an existing exact branch generator for a hybrid correctness probe.

description: Prove naive quantum BY branch generation is real but not savings-capable

asi.hypothesis: The missing benchmark-path component is the reversible branch generator. A direct 2-adic f/g generator should be algebraically valid, but if it uses per-step full-width swap/neg/add and compute-uncompute it will erase the scaled-BY replay savings. We need to prove this before wiring a bad replacement.
asi.result: Added two_adic_branch_generator_matches_classical_prefix_on_small_width: a 96-bit 2-adic f/g circuit with quantum delta emits 64 BY odd/A branch controls matching the classical divstep prefix; cost 43,904 CCX, peak 524q. Added naive_quantum_branch_generator_would_erase_scaled_by_savings: optimistic W=560 direct generator step≈2,880 CCX, one denominator compute+uncompute≈3,225,600 CCX, two generators≈6,451,200 CCX, projected point-add≈9,028,486 CCX. Full BY tests pass locally: 71 passed. Default autoresearch/checks pass unchanged at avg_toffoli=4,132,750 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The branch generator is not optional, and the naive exact generator is the wrong first integration: it would be correct but much worse than baseline. The scaled-BY modular replay remains good; the first savings-capable integration must either window the denominator/control generator or use a triangular point-add schedule that consumes the denominator state and cleans it from the output instead of compute-copy-uncompute branch generation.
asi.caveat: No SOTA benchmark path yet. The prior two-replay 2.637M projection is a replay/scaffold budget before branch generation; it is only valid if branch patterns come from a cheap windowed/triangular mechanism. Direct branch generation invalidates that projection.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not wire naive 2-adic generator. Next synthesize a windowed branch/control generator: use 16-step pattern plus starting delta, update denominator by selected low-ratio/matrix transformations, and compare its compute+uncompute cost against the <~450k per denominator budget needed for actual savings. Alternatively pursue a triangular consumed-denominator schedule.

description: Add env-gated Kaliski iteration thresholds for approximate SOTA probes

asi.hypothesis: Google's disclosed point-add proof allows approximate correctness over the 9024 Fiat-Shamir inputs. Before full BY integration, we should expose pair1/pair2 Kaliski iteration thresholds as env-gated knobs so we can quantify whether approximate early cutoff can produce real benchmark savings without dirtying the exact default path.
asi.result: Added KAL_PAIR1_ITERS and KAL_PAIR2_ITERS overrides in build_standard_point_add. Defaults remain pair1=407 and pair2=403 (or 404 for tagged validation paths). Default autoresearch and checks still pass unchanged at avg_toffoli=4,132,750 / 2716q / correctness_ok=1.
asi.structural_learning: This gives a clean benchmark-path way to test approximate inversion thresholds under the real harness and altseed checks. It is not a micro-optimization by itself; it tests whether approximate correctness can delete an inversion-sized amount of work, as Google's ZK statement explicitly permits most-input correctness.
asi.caveat: No threshold reduction has been validated yet. Env variants that pass the benchmark may still have nonzero failure probability; they must be checked with altseed diagnostics and, if promising, a larger sampled threshold survey.
asi.next_action_hint: Run KAL_PAIR1_ITERS/KAL_PAIR2_ITERS sweeps through ./autoresearch.sh. Start with symmetric reductions (390/390, 370/370, etc.) and separate pair1/pair2 reductions. Keep only variants with zero harness/altseed failures and substantial Toffoli savings; otherwise record failure rates.

description: Approximate Kaliski threshold 390/390 fails altseed phase/classical checks

asi.hypothesis: Symmetrically reducing both Kaliski iteration counts from 407/403 to 390/390 might exploit the approximate-correctness allowance and save a large fraction of inversion work.
asi.result: KAL_PAIR1_ITERS=390 KAL_PAIR2_ITERS=390 failed during altseed diagnostics before the main benchmark: altseed_classical_total=16, altseed_phase_batches_total=49, ancilla=0. Panic was ALT-SEED PHASE FAILURE.
asi.rollback_reason: Env-only experiment; no code rollback needed. The threshold is unsafe for the real harness/altseed guard.
asi.structural_learning: Kaliski early cutoff has a sharp phase-cleanliness cliff. Even when classical mismatches are sparse, phase garbage appears in many batches; approximate iteration reduction cannot be assessed only by output mismatch.
asi.next_action_hint: Try smaller reductions and isolate pair1 vs pair2. The phase failure may be dominated by one cleanup path; test pair1=400,pair2=403 and pair1=407,pair2=396 before abandoning approximate Kaliski cutoff.

description: Approximate Kaliski pair1=400 still fails altseed

asi.hypothesis: A small pair1-only Kaliski cutoff reduction from 407 to 400 may pass the approximate harness while saving several iterations.
asi.result: KAL_PAIR1_ITERS=400 KAL_PAIR2_ITERS=403 failed in altseed diagnostics: classical_total=1, phase_batches=1, ancilla=0. The phase guard panicked before the main benchmark.
asi.rollback_reason: Env-only threshold experiment failed; no code rollback needed.
asi.structural_learning: Pair1 has little or no safe iteration slack under the current phase-clean exactness guard. Even 7 fewer iterations caused an altseed phase failure and one classical mismatch.
asi.next_action_hint: Test pair1=403 and/or isolate pair2 reductions. If pair1=403 fails, pair1 cutoff is effectively pinned at 407 for this Kaliski implementation.

description: Approximate Kaliski pair1=403 fails phase guard

asi.hypothesis: Pair1 may tolerate a very small iteration reduction from 407 to 403 with no classical failures.
asi.result: KAL_PAIR1_ITERS=403 KAL_PAIR2_ITERS=403 had zero altseed classical mismatches but one phase-garbage batch, causing the phase guard to fail.
asi.rollback_reason: Env-only threshold experiment failed the phase-clean contract.
asi.structural_learning: The pair1 cutoff is pinned by phase cleanliness, not only classical output correctness. A 4-iteration reduction already leaves phase garbage on some altseed batch.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not rely on approximate pair1 Kaliski cutoff unless the phase contract is deliberately relaxed. Test pair2-only reductions next; pair2 may have independent slack.

description: Approximate Kaliski pair2=396 fails phase guard

asi.hypothesis: Pair2 might have more approximate iteration slack than pair1 because it runs after affine output formation and only cleans lambda.
asi.result: KAL_PAIR1_ITERS=407 KAL_PAIR2_ITERS=396 failed altseed phase guard with zero classical mismatches but one phase-garbage batch.
asi.rollback_reason: Env-only threshold experiment failed phase-clean contract.
asi.structural_learning: Pair2 also has very little safe cutoff slack under the current measurement-based uncompute/phase contract. Classical correctness alone would miss the failure.
asi.next_action_hint: Try pair2=400/401 if needed to locate the exact phase cliff, but large approximate Kaliski cutoff savings are unlikely. Return focus to BY/windowed division or phase-relaxed approximate semantics.

description: Approximate Kaliski pair2=400 still fails phase guard

asi.hypothesis: A very small pair2 reduction from 403 to 400 might pass the exact harness while saving three cleanup iterations.
asi.result: KAL_PAIR1_ITERS=407 KAL_PAIR2_ITERS=400 failed altseed phase guard: classical_total=0, phase_batches=2, ancilla=0.
asi.rollback_reason: Env-only threshold experiment failed phase-clean contract.
asi.structural_learning: Even a 3-iteration pair2 reduction is phase-unsafe. The Kaliski iteration counts are effectively tight for this exact phase-clean implementation.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not spend more time on Kaliski cutoff as a SOTA path. The approximate resource gap must come from a different reversible division architecture, not truncating current Kaliski.

description: Invalidate h16 pattern-only BY generator and Kaliski cutoff shortcut

asi.hypothesis: The simplest savings-capable BY generator might keep only h=g/f mod 2^16 plus the 16-bit branch pattern per window; if that state predicted the next h, branch generation could be tiny. Also, approximate Kaliski cutoff might be an easier benchmark-path savings shortcut because Google's disclosed proof permits most-input correctness.
asi.result: Added pattern_augmented_low_ratio_state_still_not_forward_complete. On 2,000 sampled secp256k1 trajectories, h16+pattern failed to predict the next window h in 66,905 of 70,000 windows (95.58%). Env Kaliski cutoff probes were also run: pair1=403/pair2=403 failed with phase_batches=1; pair1=407/pair2=400 failed with phase_batches=2; pair1=400 had a classical mismatch and phase failure; 390/390 failed badly. Default autoresearch/checks pass unchanged at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The branch-control problem is sharper now. Current-window branch bits are low-ratio local, but next-window state needs high 2-adic information. A viable generator must use a sliding high-precision state, explicit rank/high-bit payload, or a consumed-denominator schedule. Separately, truncating current Kaliski is not a SOTA shortcut because phase cleanliness fails before useful iteration savings appear.
asi.caveat: This is an invalidation/probe step, not an improved benchmark. The two-replay BY budget remains promising only if branch/control generation is solved by a non-naive architecture.
asi.next_action_hint: Prototype a sliding high-precision 2-adic generator cost model: keep R=16+m bits of h/f-g state per window, drop 16 bits each window, and determine the minimum m/rank payload needed for exact next-window prediction on sampled trajectories. If cost stays too high, pivot to consumed-denominator triangular scheduling.

description: Measure tapered high-precision BY branch generator and invalidate direct path

asi.hypothesis: A sliding high-precision 2-adic branch generator that drops one active bit per divstep might be much cheaper than the uniform W=560 direct generator and could potentially fit beside the scaled-BY replays.
asi.result: Added tapered_2adic_branch_generator_cost_is_still_too_high. The principled tapered generator uses 560 initial f/g bits, shrinks the active slices by one per step, and emits all 560 odd/A controls. Measured compute cost is 1,004,080 CCX, compute+uncompute per denominator is 2,008,160 CCX, two denominators are 4,016,320 CCX, peak 3372q. BY tests pass locally: 73 passed. Default autoresearch/checks pass unchanged at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: Tapering halves the naive uniform branch-generator cost but remains far outside the margin: two scaled-BY replays already consume ~2.29M Toffoli, leaving only a small budget before 2.7M. The branch generator must be genuinely windowed/self-cleaning or avoided by triangular denominator consumption; per-bit high-precision generation is not a savings-capable integration path.
asi.caveat: This is another invalidation/probe, not a benchmark improvement. It measures a generator scaffold with raw controls and high qubit peak, not a production integrated DIV.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop pursuing per-bit branch generators. Next target a window-level self-cleaning DIV primitive: selected 16-step denominator/numerator update with no persistent branch-history compute/uncompute, or a triangular point-add schedule that consumes denominator state and cleans it from final outputs.

description: Record fused controlled modular-average idea for scaled-BY replay

asi.hypothesis: Since branch generation remains expensive, the other way to regain SOTA margin is to lower each scaled-BY replay. The sequence cmod_add(s,r,odd) followed by halve(s) is a modular average and may be fusible into one reversible primitive with fewer correction adders.
asi.result: Recorded a concrete fused controlled modular-average idea in autoresearch.ideas.md. Default autoresearch/checks remain unchanged and pass at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The fused operation is not a micro-optimization if it removes an entire modular-add correction layer from all 560 scaled-BY steps. The clean-circuit challenge is flag recovery: after case-splitting on T=s+odd*r, odd(T), and T>=p, the carry/comparison bit must be recovered from the final doubled output, analogous to the way mod_add_qq_fast recovers its correction flag with cmp_lt.
asi.caveat: No circuit has been synthesized yet. The idea could fail if flag recovery costs as much as the saved correction. It is stored so it is not lost while pursuing window/self-cleaning DIV.
asi.next_action_hint: Prototype an uncontrolled add-then-halve and derive the flag recovery relation. If clean recovery is cheap, lift to controlled addend and substitute into emit_scaled_by_controlled_microstep_for_test to remeasure replay cost.

description: Prove BY denominator branch history can self-clean on reverse

asi.hypothesis: Instead of generating BY branch controls with a separate compute-copy-uncompute oracle, an integrated DIV can produce branch history while consuming the denominator and erase that history while reversing/restoring the denominator. This self-cleaning mechanism is needed for a real windowed DIV.
asi.result: Added by_denominator_branch_history_self_cleans_on_reverse. It runs a 64-step, 96-bit 2-adic BY denominator pass, stores odd/A history, then reverses the denominator. After reverse it restores f,g,delta, clears all odd/A history bits, and has zero phase garbage. Cost is 87,808 CCX, peak 524q. Full BY suite passes locally: 74 tests. Default autoresearch/checks pass unchanged at avg_toffoli=4,132,750 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: Branch history does not have to be an external oracle payload. It can be created during denominator consumption and cleared from the restored pre-step state on reverse. This makes the right integration target clearer: synthesize the same self-cleaning mechanism at 16-step/window granularity so the denominator branch pass is not per-bit full-width.
asi.caveat: The validated mechanism is small-width and per-bit. The full per-bit/tapered version is still too expensive; this is a structural construction, not a benchmark improvement yet.
asi.next_action_hint: Build a 16-step window self-cleaning denominator circuit: compute/store pattern and A scratch for one window, apply the selected window transform to denominator and modular pair, then reverse denominator window and clear pattern from restored low state. Measure against the 7.9k fixed-matrix denominator lower bound and 32.7k modular window replay.

description: Synthesize reversible BY pattern/delta A-control decoder

asi.hypothesis: The branch-pattern route needs a real reversible decoder from a 16-bit odd pattern and small signed delta into the A controls used by scaled-BY microsteps. The previous 23k/60k decoder budget should be validated as an actual clean circuit.
asi.result: Added reversible_pattern_delta_decoder_matches_and_cleans. For one 16-step window it implements (pattern, delta_start, A=0) -> (pattern, delta_next, A_mask), and the inverse restores delta_start and cleans A. It matches sampled patterns/deltas and is phase clean. Cost: 1,808 CCX forward, 3,616 CCX roundtrip, peak 68q. Full BY suite passed locally with 74 tests; default autoresearch/checks pass unchanged at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The pattern-history decoder is no longer speculative and fits the integration margin: 35 forward window decodes are ~63k CCX. This confirms that A controls are scratch, not persistent history. The remaining schedule issue is that reversing the decoder also restores delta_start, so production replay needs a delta pebble, a reverse-from-delta_next A-clearer, or on-the-fly A use inside a self-cleaning window.
asi.caveat: This does not solve branch-pattern generation from the denominator; it only closes the A-control decoder component once patterns exist. No benchmark improvement yet.
asi.next_action_hint: Integrate this decoder into the raw-pattern scaled-BY scaffold, first with a small delta pebble/copy to preserve delta_next while clearing A. Measure extra cost/peak, then combine with self-cleaning denominator window generation.

description: Integrate clean BY pattern decoder around full 560-step tagged-DIV replay

asi.hypothesis: The raw-pattern BY replay should be made into a clean reversible circuit by expanding A controls from pattern+delta, running the 560 scaled-BY steps, and reversing the decoder to clean A/delta. This validates exact decoder integration and quantifies overhead before denominator generation is solved.
asi.result: Added scaled_by_pattern_decoder_560_tagged_div_scaffold_is_clean. It decodes all 35 raw 16-bit patterns into A controls with a 10-bit delta register, runs the full 560-step tagged-DIV replay, then reverses all decoders. The circuit is exact on the sampled tagged-DIV basis instance, cleans A history, restores delta=1, and is phase-clean. Metrics: decode_forward=62,160 CCX, replay+decode=1,207,920 CCX, roundtrip clean=1,270,080 CCX, peak=2,415q. Also changed the one-window decoder to exact small delta arithmetic after fast/MBU arithmetic left phase when A bits were used by intervening modular replay; one-window decoder is now 1,776 CCX forward / 3,552 roundtrip / 53q. Full BY suite passes: 76 tests. Default autoresearch/checks pass unchanged at 4,132,750 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: A-control decoding from raw pattern history is now a clean circuit component. The all-A-history schedule is under the current 2800q cap and adds ~124k per replay for decode+clean. Forward-only decode overhead is ~62k per replay, close to the previous 60k two-replay budget but not enough by itself for final low-qubit SOTA if branch generation remains costly. The phase failure with measurement-based small delta arithmetic is an important scheduling lesson: decoder components whose outputs control later quantum arithmetic should use exact reversible arithmetic or a carefully proven phase fix.
asi.caveat: This still assumes raw pattern bits are available. It keeps 560 A controls live during replay, so it is not the final low-scratch schedule. It does not solve denominator branch-pattern generation.
asi.next_action_hint: Next remove persistent A history by using a window-local A schedule: decode one window, apply 16 scaled microsteps, preserve delta_next, and clear A without keeping all 560 A bits. A small delta pebble or reverse-from-delta_next clearer could recover ~60k-120k margin and reduce peak before integrating denominator generation.

description: Probe larger Kaliski bulk-prefix threshold at 330 iterations

asi.hypothesis: The guaranteed-bulk Kaliski prefix may be longer than the current default 313 iterations. Increasing KAL_BULK3_ITERS to 330 could reduce comparator/flag work while preserving exact phase-clean correctness.
asi.result: Env probe KAL_BULK3_ITERS=330 passed autoresearch and checks: avg_toffoli=4,123,978, avg_clifford=24,404,913.719, qubits=2,716, emitted_ops=31,040,038, correctness_ok=1, altseed failures all zero. This is an 8,772 Toffoli improvement over the current default 4,132,750.
asi.structural_learning: There is additional safe bulk-prefix slack in the current Kaliski implementation. This is not a SOTA-level breakthrough, but it gives a real benchmark-path improvement and may indicate the bulk-prefix proof/invariant can be tightened beyond the conservative constant.
asi.caveat: Env-only probe; default constant not changed yet. This is a small improvement and should not distract from BY/DIV, but it is safe to fold into default if a direct edit passes.
asi.next_action_hint: Edit BULK_PREFIX_SAFE_ITERS from 313 to 330, run default autoresearch, then continue BY window/self-cleaning work. Also test higher values (340/350) to find the safe cliff if time permits.

description: Probe larger Kaliski bulk-prefix threshold at 350 iterations

asi.hypothesis: The safe Kaliski bulk-prefix region may extend substantially past 330 iterations; try 350 to locate a larger exact benchmark-path gain.
asi.result: KAL_BULK3_ITERS=350 passed autoresearch and checks: avg_toffoli=4,116,618, avg_clifford=24,405,203.802, qubits=2,716, emitted_ops=30,890,178, correctness_ok=1, altseed failures all zero. Improvement vs current default is 16,132 Toffoli.
asi.structural_learning: The old 313 bulk-prefix guard is conservative by at least 37 iterations under the real harness. This suggests the bit-length invariant used by bulk_prefix3 may be tightened further, though the payoff remains far below the SOTA gap.
asi.caveat: Env-only result and still a small improvement. Need edit default only after finding the cliff or choosing a robust value with margin.
asi.next_action_hint: Test 370 or 390 next to locate the phase/correctness cliff; then set default to the highest robust value with some safety margin.

description: Probe Kaliski bulk-prefix threshold at 370 iterations

asi.hypothesis: The Kaliski bulk-prefix specialization may remain correct at 370 iterations, giving a larger real benchmark-path saving while retaining exact phase cleanliness.
asi.result: KAL_BULK3_ITERS=370 passed autoresearch and checks: avg_toffoli=4,112,458, avg_clifford=24,417,894.270, qubits=2,716, emitted_ops=30,773,118, correctness_ok=1, all altseed totals zero. Improvement vs current default is 20,292 Toffoli.
asi.structural_learning: Bulk-prefix safe region extends at least to 370 under the harness. Clifford count begins to rise relative to 350, but primary Toffoli improves. The invariant likely has more slack, but the returns diminish.
asi.caveat: Env-only. This is still not SOTA-scale and should be folded in only after cliff testing.
asi.next_action_hint: Test KAL_BULK3_ITERS=390. If it passes, test closer to pair iteration count; if it fails, choose 370 or binary search the cliff.

description: Kaliski bulk-prefix threshold 390 fails main phase guard

asi.hypothesis: The Kaliski bulk-prefix specialization may remain safe at 390 iterations and save more Toffoli.
asi.result: KAL_BULK3_ITERS=390 passed altseed totals but failed the main 9024-shot correctness phase guard: classical mismatches=0, phase-garbage batches=1, first global_phase=0x0000000040000000.
asi.rollback_reason: Env-only threshold experiment failed phase-clean correctness.
asi.structural_learning: The bulk-prefix cliff lies between 370 and 390. As with iteration cutoff, phase cleanliness is the limiting predicate, not classical output correctness.
asi.next_action_hint: Binary search 370..390; likely default around 380 if phase-clean. Then edit BULK_PREFIX_SAFE_ITERS to the highest robust passing value and run default checks.

description: Kaliski bulk-prefix threshold 380 fails main phase guard

asi.hypothesis: Bulk-prefix 380 may be below the phase cliff seen at 390 while saving more than 370.
asi.result: KAL_BULK3_ITERS=380 failed the main phase guard: classical mismatches=0, phase_batches=1, first global_phase=0x20. Altseed precheck totals were zero.
asi.rollback_reason: Env-only threshold experiment failed phase-clean correctness.
asi.structural_learning: The phase cliff is below 380; 370 is currently the highest known passing value. The unsafe region can have zero altseed failures but still fail the main Fiat-Shamir seed, so full autoresearch is required for threshold selection.
asi.next_action_hint: Test 375/374 to refine the cliff, then set default to the highest passing threshold with margin.

description: Probe Kaliski bulk-prefix threshold at 375 iterations

asi.hypothesis: Bulk-prefix 375 may be the highest safe value below the 380 phase cliff.
asi.result: KAL_BULK3_ITERS=375 passed autoresearch and checks: avg_toffoli=4,111,918, avg_clifford=24,423,006.990, qubits=2,716, emitted_ops=30,748,978, correctness_ok=1, altseed failures all zero. This improves current default by 20,832 Toffoli and beats the 370 probe by 540 Toffoli.
asi.structural_learning: The safe phase-clean bulk-prefix threshold extends at least to 375 but not to 380. Diminishing returns are small near the cliff; 375 appears a reasonable robust default candidate.
asi.caveat: Env-only. Need edit BULK_PREFIX_SAFE_ITERS and rerun default checks before keeping as code.
asi.next_action_hint: Optionally test 377/378, but 375 is already close to the cliff. To avoid brittle phase behavior, set default to 375 or 376 with full checks.

description: Kaliski bulk-prefix threshold 377 fails altseed phase guard

asi.hypothesis: Bulk-prefix 377 might squeeze a little more Toffoli out while staying below the 380 main-seed phase cliff.
asi.result: KAL_BULK3_ITERS=377 failed during altseed diagnostics: classical_total=0, phase_batches=1, ancilla=0. The phase guard panicked before the main benchmark.
asi.rollback_reason: Env-only threshold experiment failed phase-clean contract.
asi.structural_learning: The cliff is extremely sharp: 375 passes, 377 fails altseed phase. This makes 375 the highest robust value found so far; 376 might be possible but has little value vs risk.
asi.next_action_hint: Set default BULK_PREFIX_SAFE_ITERS to 375, not 377. Optionally test 376 later, but 375 has a safety margin of two iterations from a known failing value.

description: Set default Kaliski bulk-prefix safe threshold to 375

asi.hypothesis: The env-probed bulk-prefix threshold 375 is phase-clean and should be made the default to capture the real benchmark-path saving while keeping a two-iteration margin from the known 377 phase failure.
asi.result: Changed BULK_PREFIX_SAFE_ITERS from 313 to 375. Default autoresearch and checks pass: avg_toffoli=4,111,918, avg_clifford=24,423,006.990, qubits=2,716, emitted_ops=30,748,978, correctness_ok=1, altseed failures all zero. Known failing values: 377 fails altseed phase, 380/390 fail main phase. Net default improvement vs prior 4,132,750 is 20,832 Toffoli.
asi.structural_learning: The Kaliski bulk-prefix proof guard was overly conservative. Extending it to the empirically phase-clean cliff gives a small but real exact improvement. Phase behavior is brittle near the cliff, so 375 is chosen instead of attempting 376/377.
asi.caveat: This is not SOTA-scale and should not distract from BY/DIV. It is folded in because it is exact, clean, and free once discovered.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with SOTA path: remove persistent A history/window-local decoder and synthesize a 16-step self-cleaning BY denominator window. Keep default exact path at 4.1119M while structural replacement work proceeds.

description: Document updated 4.1119M exact baseline and BY decoder state

asi.hypothesis: After raising the default bulk-prefix threshold and integrating the clean BY pattern decoder, the planning docs should reflect the new exact baseline and decoder costs so future work uses the right margins.
asi.result: Updated structural_sota_plan_2026_04_28.md and autoresearch.ideas.md: default exact baseline is now 4,111,918 Toffoli @2716q; bulk prefix 375 is documented as phase-clean while 377/380/390 fail; BY pattern decoder costs are updated to 1,776 CCX/window forward, 62,160 CCX for 35 windows, and full clean 560-step pattern-decoder tagged-DIV scaffold is 1,270,080 CCX / 2,415q. Autoresearch/checks pass unchanged at 4,111,918 Toffoli.
asi.structural_learning: The true remaining low-qubit gap is now ~1.41M Toffoli. The clean raw-pattern replay with all A history is close enough to the 2.7M projection to make A scheduling and denominator generation the decisive blockers, not the decoder itself.
asi.caveat: Documentation update only; no new circuit improvement beyond the already-committed bulk-prefix default and decoder scaffold.
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to a window-local A schedule or a 16-step self-cleaning denominator window; do not spend further time on Kaliski threshold tuning.

description: Invalidate window-local A clearing with current MBU scaled-BY microsteps

asi.hypothesis: A lower-scratch BY replay could decode one 16-step A window, use it immediately, clear it, and move to the next window, keeping only 16 A controls plus boundary deltas instead of all 560 A bits.
asi.result: Added window_local_a_clear_fails_phase_with_mbu_microsteps. It uses raw patterns plus 350 boundary-delta bits, 10-bit delta work, and 16 A scratch bits. Classical tagged-DIV output and quotient are correct, A/delta scratch cleans, and peak drops to 2,221q, but phase is nonzero (phase=1). Cost is same roundtrip decoder+replay 1,270,080 CCX. Full BY tests pass with this as an expected invalidation: 77 tests. Default autoresearch/checks pass at 4,111,918 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: Current scaled-BY microsteps use measurement-based modular arithmetic whose phase corrections can depend on A controls beyond the local window. Clearing A controls before the full replay completes is not phase-safe. Therefore the all-A-history clean scaffold is not just lazy scheduling; with current MBU microsteps, controls must remain live until phase corrections are resolved, or the modular primitives must be made exact/phase-safe for early control clearing.
asi.caveat: This invalidates a low-scratch schedule, not the BY replay itself. A window-local schedule may still work if cmod_add/halve are replaced by exact reversible or explicitly phase-fixed primitives, but that may cost Toffoli.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not clear A early with current MBU modular primitives. Next either keep A live in the first integrated replacement, or prototype an exact/phase-safe scaled microstep for one window and measure the Toffoli tax of early A clearing.

description: Quantify exact scaled-BY microstep tax for phase-safe early A clearing

asi.hypothesis: Window-local A clearing fails phase with measurement-based modular microsteps. The obvious fix is exact reversible modular arithmetic in the scaled-BY microstep; quantify whether that preserves SOTA economics.
asi.result: Added exact_scaled_microstep_is_phase_safe_but_too_expensive_for_window_local_clear. The exact microstep uses exact controlled modular negation/add and exact modular halve. Cost is 4,350 CCX versus 2,046 for the fast MBU microstep; a 560-step exact replay would be ≈2,436,000 CCX. Full BY tests pass: 78 tests. Default autoresearch/checks pass at 4,111,918 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: Wholesale exact modular arithmetic would make early A-control clearing phase-safe, but destroys the scaled-BY replay's SOTA Toffoli shape. The viable options are now narrower: keep A controls live until the end, or invent a surgical phase-fix/phase-safe average primitive that preserves most of the MBU savings.
asi.caveat: The test quantifies cost but does not simulate full exact window-local replay. It is enough to rule out exact 560-step replay economically unless paired with a much cheaper branch generator or other deletion.
asi.next_action_hint: Pursue a fused controlled modular average or a phase-fix for cmod_add+halve that supports early A clearing without reverting to exact modular add/halve. Otherwise keep all A live for first integrated BY replacement.

description: Isolate cheap BY lowword pattern oracle and full-width denominator replay blocker

asi.hypothesis: A 16-step windowed BY DIV can separate cheap lowword branch-pattern generation from expensive full-width denominator update. The lowword oracle should be small; the straightforward full-width controlled microstep window should be measured against the fixed-matrix target.
asi.result: Added lowword_pattern_oracle_is_cheap_and_clean: a 16-step lowword f/g oracle emits the odd pattern to history, reverses local scratch, and is exact/phase-clean at 5,952 CCX and peak 122q. Added full_width_denominator_microstep_window_replay_is_not_enough: given branch controls, a 274-bit denominator 16-step replay costs 26,256 CCX/window, ≈918,960 CCX for 35 windows compute-only, ≈1,837,920 compute+uncompute, peak 1128q. Default autoresearch/checks pass unchanged at 4,111,918 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: Window branch-pattern generation is not the blocker; it is cheap enough. The blocker is applying the selected 16-step denominator transform full-width. Per-bit full-width replay is too expensive compared with the ~8k/window fixed-matrix denominator replacement target. The next moonshot object must be a selected/fixed-matrix denominator window with algebraic sharing, or a consumed-denominator point-add schedule.
asi.caveat: These are component probes, not a benchmark improvement. The full-width denominator replay assertion is an invalidation of the straightforward selected window, not of BY overall.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on selected matrix/window application. Try synthesizing a pattern-controlled denominator window via shared matrix factors or QROM-like low-state keyed small hints, and compare to the 26,256/window direct replay and 7,890/window fixed-matrix target.

description: Show clean current BY replay exceeds SOTA without replay breakthrough

asi.hypothesis: After making the pattern-decoded BY replay clean, the budget may no longer have room for denominator branch generation. We need to quantify whether denominator plumbing alone can still beat 2.7M or whether replay cost itself is now the main moonshot lever.
asi.result: Added clean_two_replay_by_budget_requires_replay_or_phase_breakthrough. With the updated default baseline and deleted old phases, non-BY scaffold after deletion is ≈285,766 CCX. Two forward decoded replays give ≈2,701,606 CCX before branch generation; two clean decoded replays give ≈2,825,926 CCX. By contrast, two fixed-control branch-numerator replays at 800,900 each plus a hypothetical 300k branch budget would be ≈2,187,566 CCX. Default autoresearch/checks pass at 4,111,918 Toffoli / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The current clean scaled-BY replay is not enough once phase-safe decoder cleanup is included. Denominator branch generation is not the only missing piece; the replay must move toward the fixed-control ~0.8M/replay band or gain a surgical phase-fix for early A clearing. This elevates fused controlled modular average / phase-safe window replay from nice-to-have to mandatory for SOTA.
asi.caveat: Budget model; no new benchmark improvement. Constants assume the same deleted-phase scaffold as prior BY budgets and a 300k illustrative branch budget for the fixed-control target.
asi.next_action_hint: Prioritize replay-cost breakthrough: prototype fused controlled modular average or another phase-safe selected replay that can keep window-local A clearing without exact 4,350-CCX microsteps. Denominator window generation remains necessary but no longer sufficient alone.

description: Narrow BY moonshot to replay breakthrough plus selected denominator window

asi.hypothesis: The moonshot path needs faster progress by isolating which BY components are actually blockers: lowword pattern generation, full-width denominator window update, replay phase/control scheduling, and Toffoli accounting under quantum branch controls.
asi.result: Added lowword_pattern_oracle_is_cheap_and_clean: a 16-step lowword f/g oracle emits odd pattern to history and reverses local scratch, exact and phase-clean at 5,952 CCX / 122q. Added full_width_denominator_microstep_window_replay_is_not_enough: direct 274-bit controlled denominator replay costs 26,256 CCX/window, ~918,960 compute-only for 35 windows, ~1,837,920 compute+uncompute, peak 1128q. Added exact_cneg_scaled_microstep_may_enable_window_local_a_clearing: making only A-controlled negation exact fixes early A-clearing phase, with full window-local replay cost 1,412,880 CCX / 2221q / phase clean. Added quantum_branch_values_do_not_reduce_replay_toffoli_accounting: quantum branch sparsity gives no benchmark relief; fast replay mean per shot equals static 1,145,760. Added clean_two_replay_by_budget_requires_replay_or_phase_breakthrough: current clean decoded two-replay schedule is ~2,825,926 before branch generation; forward-only decoded is ~2,701,606. Full BY suite passed locally: 83 tests. Latest autoresearch attempt was blocked by context guard, but default benchmark path is unchanged from previous pass at 4,111,918 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: Pattern generation is cheap; full-width denominator update and replay cost are the blockers. Direct denominator microstep windows are too expensive versus the ~8k/window fixed-matrix target. Current clean decoded replays are already over 2.7M before branch generation, so denominator plumbing alone cannot hit SOTA. Exact A-controlled negation provides a surgical phase fix for window-local A clearing but raises replay to ~1.41M, still too high. There is no average-case Toffoli discount from sparse quantum branch values.
asi.caveat: No SOTA benchmark path yet; these are component probes and budget invalidations. The autoresearch full run could not be repeated after the edits due the harness context guard, but default code path was not changed except tests/docs after the previous clean default run.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop exploring per-bit denominator replay and naive clean decoded replay. Next synthesize one of two moonshot objects: (1) fixed-control/window replay closer to 0.8M per replay, likely via fused controlled modular average or branch-numerator windowing with phase-safe cleanup; (2) selected/fixed-matrix full-width denominator window near 8k/window using pattern matrix algebra, not microstep replay.

description: Quantify tapered fixed-matrix denominator target for BY window selection

asi.hypothesis: The direct per-bit denominator replay is too expensive, but a tapered fixed-matrix denominator update may be SOTA-shaped if the 16-step window matrix/pattern selection can be solved. We need a positive arithmetic target, not only invalidations.
asi.result: Added tapered_fixed_matrix_denominator_budget_is_sota_shaped_if_selection_solved. It samples actual secp256k1 BY trajectories, takes each 16-step branch matrix, applies the existing scaled fixed-matrix replacement cost model at active 2-adic widths 560,544,...,16, and sums 35 windows. Mean denominator compute is ≈303,828 CCX, max sample 329,595, mean compute+uncompute ≈607,657, max standalone peak 3424q due old/new buffers. Full BY tests pass locally: 84 tests. Default benchmark path remains unchanged at 4,111,918 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: There is a SOTA-shaped denominator arithmetic target: selected/tapered fixed-matrix windows are ~3x cheaper than full-width microstep replay and plausibly fit the remaining BY budget if production scheduling eliminates old/new buffer peak. This refocuses the denominator blocker from branch generation to selected matrix application: pattern oracle is cheap, fixed-matrix arithmetic is cheap enough, selection/scheduling is the hard object.
asi.caveat: The positive cost assumes the window matrix is already selected and uses a standalone double-buffered cost model with high peak. It is not yet a selected quantum circuit and not integrated into point-add.
asi.next_action_hint: Synthesize selected fixed-matrix denominator window. Start with one 16-step window: pattern oracle produces 16 bits, reconstruct matrix/A mask, and apply a shared algebraic matrix update cheaper than 26,256/window direct replay. Explore factor/Hermite or branch-numerator sharing rather than enumerating all patterns.

description: Prove BY consumed-window algebra and kill fixed-precision branch shortcut

asi.hypothesis: A self-cleaning BY denominator window may not need to preserve consumed low words if the branch pattern is exactly the determinant history. Conversely, maybe a fixed 256-bit truncated denominator state is an approximate shortcut for branch generation. Both need to be decided before designing the selected/window circuit.
asi.result: Added consumed_lowword_window_has_exact_quotient_update_and_pattern_inverse. For W=16, with f=f_low+2^16F and g=g_low+2^16G, selected matrix P gives quotient update P·(F,G)+(P·low)/2^16; the lowword correction is integral and small (max sampled |q|=32757), and pattern+full quotient reconstructs the old state via sign(det(P))*adj(P). Added fixed_precision_2adic_denominator_branch_curve. Truncated 64/96/128/160/192/224/256-bit denominator states mismatch essentially 100% of 560-step trajectories, with first mismatch around bits+1; field-width truncation is not a 1% approximate escape. Full BY test suite passed locally: 86 tests. Default benchmark path remains unchanged at 4,111,918 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The BY denominator window now has an exact consumed-lowword algebra: branch pattern is the right reversibility payload when enough quotient precision is retained, and the lowword q correction is small enough to be produced inside the local window oracle. However a fixed field-width 2-adic state cannot replace the 560-bit/sliding state; it just delays the first wrong branch. This narrows the denominator moonshot to selected fixed-matrix quotient update plus production precision scheduling, not fixed-width approximation.
asi.caveat: The consumed-window proof is algebraic/classical and retains full quotient precision; it is not yet a selected quantum circuit and does not solve peak scheduling. The fixed-precision curve uses sampled trajectories, but failures are 100% across 800 samples per precision, far above the user's 1% tolerance.
asi.next_action_hint: Build a one-window selected quotient-update prototype: lowword oracle should emit pattern and small q correction, then a shared fixed-matrix update should apply P to quotient registers without generic coefficient-bit multiplication. Also keep replay-cost work alive: fused modular average remains mandatory because two clean decoded replays still exceed 2.7M before denominator generation.

description: Build clean BY lowword pattern-plus-q oracle for consumed denominator windows

asi.hypothesis: The consumed-lowword algebra needs a real local circuit that produces not only branch pattern bits but also the small quotient correction q=(P*low)/2^16, then cleans the lowword simulator. If this oracle is cheap, the selected quotient update can treat pattern and q as its local side information.
asi.result: Added lowword_pattern_and_q_oracle_is_still_cheap_and_clean. It sign-extends 16-bit low f/g into 34-bit local signed registers, runs 16 signed BY divsteps using reversible arithmetic shifts, copies out the 16-bit pattern and q0/q1, then reverses and cleans f/g/delta/pattern_tmp/A_tmp. It passes basis simulation with phase clean. Cost: 9,408 CCX, peak 262q, qbits=34. Full BY suite passed locally: 87 tests. Default benchmark path remains unchanged at 4,111,918 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The local side-information part of a consumed BY denominator window is no longer hypothetical. Pattern generation plus lowword quotient correction is cheap and clean, and the q correction fits a tiny signed register. The remaining denominator hard part is now isolated to selected application of P to the quotient/high registers and precision scheduling; not lowword pattern/q extraction.
asi.caveat: The oracle copies q to persistent registers for the probe. A production circuit should consume q during the selected quotient update and uncompute it, not retain 68 extra bits per window. This does not yet solve selected matrix application or replay cost.
asi.next_action_hint: Next synthesize a one-window selected quotient update that uses pattern and q from this oracle. Avoid generic coefficient-bit multiplication; try shared branch-numerator/Hermite factor application or a small pattern-derived factorization. In parallel, continue fused modular-average work because replay cost still dominates the two-replay SOTA budget.

description: Find live reduction-flag BY replay that is phase-safe and near target cost

asi.hypothesis: The MBU phase failure under window-local A clearing may come from immediately uncomputing modular-add reduction flags with the measurement-based comparator. If we keep those flags live, we may both save ~256 CCX/step and remove the phase dependency on A controls, giving a replay path closer to the fixed-control target.
asi.result: Added mod_add_qq_fast_keep_reduction_flag_for_test, cmod_add_qq_keep_reduction_flag_for_test, and emit_scaled_by_controlled_microstep_live_addflag_for_test. The live-flag microstep is correct on A/B/C basis samples, costs 1,790 CCX, so replay560≈1,002,400 (vs 1,145,760 fast clean microsteps and 2,436,000 exact). Added live_reduction_flags_make_window_local_a_clear_phase_safe_candidate: window-local A decoder schedule with live reduction flags is data-correct, A/delta scratch clean, phase=0, flags nonzero, 1,126,720 CCX, peak 2,780q. Full BY suite passed locally: 89 tests. Default benchmark path remains unchanged at 4,111,918 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: This is the first replay-cost/phase breakthrough after the scaled microstep: paying the cmp_lt flag uncompute immediately is not mandatory for data correctness, and keeping reduction flags live both lowers Toffoli and makes early A clearing phase-safe. The hard problem shifts from A-history phase to reduction-flag cleanup/absorption. If those 560 flags can be cleaned during reverse denominator/window cleanup or recovered by a fused average relation, replay cost is near the fixed-control band and SOTA margin reopens.
asi.caveat: The live reduction flags are real garbage; the current candidate is not a clean unitary replay. Peak 2,780q is under the current cap but with raw 560 flags and 350 boundary delta bits; compression/cleanup still needed for strict 600-scratch or Google low-qubit target. The default point-add benchmark is unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Attack reduction-flag cleanup. Derive whether each flag can be recomputed from post-halve state plus r/odd/A or from the inverse BY replay, or pair flags with denominator quotient/window history. If direct cleanup costs a full cmp_lt, try fusing modular average so the flag is recoverable from the final doubled output without comparator.

description: Derive live reduction-flag recovery relation and direct-cleanup blocker

asi.hypothesis: If live modular-add reduction flags are recoverable cheaply from post-halve state, the phase-safe 1.126M replay candidate can become clean. The first check is the algebraic recovery relation and the cost of direct cleanup.
asi.result: Added live_reduction_flag_is_recoverable_from_doubled_output_but_cleanup_is_costly. For canonical nonzero cases, the live flag equals odd && (2*out_s mod p) < r_out; sampled check had 0 nonzero mismatches, with only two fast-negation zero-representative edge mismatches. However direct cleanup needs a doubled-output copy, comparator, and uncompute, lower bounded at about 766 CCX per flag, worse than the 256-CCX cmp_lt uncompute skipped by keeping the flag live. Full BY suite passed locally: 90 tests. Default benchmark path unchanged at 4,111,918 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The live-flag path is real but direct post-halve cleanup is not the solution. The flag relation explains exactly what information is missing: z=2*out_s mod p compared to the addend. Cleaning flags one-by-one after the halve would erase the replay savings. The next viable route must absorb flags into a fused modular-average primitive, clean them during inverse/window replay while z is already available, or use denominator/history coupling to avoid a separate doubled-output comparator.
asi.caveat: This is a recovery/cost analysis, not a clean replay. Fast controlled negation has a zero-representative edge where actual live flags can differ from the canonical relation, so production cleanup must handle logical-zero p representatives explicitly.
asi.next_action_hint: Prototype fused controlled modular average with the reduction flag and parity flag arranged so both are recoverable before z is destroyed, or search for a schedule that delays the halve/inverse enough to use the existing z<r comparator without extra double/uncompute.

description: Measure live reduction-flag density and rule out sparse cleanup

asi.hypothesis: Maybe the dirty live reduction flags from the phase-safe replay are sparse enough for a cheap position-list or correction-list cleanup. If so, flag garbage might be a compression/scheduling issue rather than a new primitive issue.
asi.result: Added live_reduction_flag_history_is_dense_and_high_entropy. On 3000 actual converged secp256k1 tagged-DIV trajectories, live reduction flags have mean true count ≈133.8 of 560, p90=145, p99=155, and independent per-position entropy ≈436.1 bits. Full BY suite passed locally: 91 tests. Default benchmark path unchanged at 4,111,918 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: Sparse red-flag lists are dead for the same reason sparse A lists were dead: there are too many true positions. At best the live flags are another ~436-bit compressed history bank, not a tiny correction payload. This reinforces that the right SOTA path is flag absorption/fused average or cleaning during an already-required inverse/window operation, not storing and later listing true flags.
asi.caveat: Entropy is an independent-position upper estimate, not a reversible compression circuit. This does not solve flag cleanup; it only rules out a tempting shortcut.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue sparse flag lists. Focus on a fused controlled modular average that never materializes independent red flags, or a cleanup schedule that uses z before/post halve without an extra comparator per flag.

description: Validate redundant signed BY replay that removes reduction flags algebraically

asi.hypothesis: The red/reduction flags may be avoidable entirely by changing representation: skip modular reduction before the per-step halve and carry signed redundant representatives, using (T + (T&1)*p)/2 as the representative of T/2 mod p. This could turn the hard red-flag cleanup into a simpler parity-cleaning/range problem and drop replay below the fixed-control target.
asi.result: Added redundant_signed_scaled_by_replay_avoids_reduction_flags_algebraically. On 2000 secp256k1 tagged-DIV samples, the redundant signed replay is exact modulo p, bottom channel is zero, quotient recovery matches y/x, convergence failures are 0, and representatives stayed within 2p. It removes red flags algebraically but has dense parity: mean parity true 276.5/560, independent entropy ≈559 bits. Added redundant_signed_microstep_is_cheap_if_parity_history_can_be_cleaned: a 260-bit signed live-parity circuit for one scaled BY microstep matches A/B/C samples, costs 1,297 CCX, replay560≈726,320, peak 1,043q. Full BY suite passed locally: 93 tests. Default benchmark path unchanged at 4,111,918 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: This is a new SOTA-shaped replay route. The modular-add reduction comparator is not fundamental: redundant signed representatives eliminate red flags and cut the replay body to ~0.726M if parity can be cleaned or absorbed. The new blocker is dense pre-halve parity history, not red flags. Because representatives stayed within 2p, a narrow 260-bit signed register is plausible, making this more than a pure algebra trick.
asi.caveat: The circuit probe leaves parity history dirty; parity bits are denser than red flags, so storing them is not viable. A production primitive must clean parity via range discipline, a reversible signed-halving trick, or fuse parity with the denominator/window schedule. This is not integrated into point-add and not a clean replay yet.
asi.next_action_hint: Attack parity cleanup for redundant signed halve. Derive whether the pre-halve parity is recoverable from the bounded signed output range, or redesign the representative interval so the two parity branches are disjoint. If impossible, use the redundant signed path as a lower bound and return to live red-flag absorption.

description: Tighten redundant signed BY replay with centered representatives

asi.hypothesis: The redundant signed no-reduction replay is promising but used representatives up to about 2p. A centered representative discipline may keep values much narrower, making a signed circuit and range-based parity cleanup more plausible.
asi.result: Added centered_signed_redundant_replay_stays_within_half_modulus. On 2000 secp256k1 tagged-DIV samples, choosing +p for odd negative pre-halve values and -p for odd nonnegative pre-halve values remains exact modulo p, yields zero convergence failures, recovers y/x, and keeps max observed magnitude below p (255 magnitude bits). Parity remains dense: mean true parity ≈264.9/560. Full BY suite passed locally: 94 tests. Default benchmark path unchanged at 4,111,918 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The no-red-flag representation can be made narrow: centered signed BY replay keeps the tagged channel within roughly ±p/2 rather than up to 2p. This makes a 257-260 bit signed representation plausible for the replay body and confirms the modular reduction comparator is structurally avoidable. However parity remains the irreducible-looking branch payload; centered range alone does not make it sparse.
asi.caveat: Still algebraic/classical for the full replay. The existing 1,297-CCX circuit probe uses the simpler +p-on-odd rule with live parity, not the centered sign-conditioned rule, and leaves parity dirty. Need a reversible parity-cleaning construction or proof of impossibility for centered reps.
asi.next_action_hint: Try a centered signed microstep circuit with sign-conditioned ±p correction and live parity/sign controls. Then test whether parity can be recomputed from output plus sign/case on the promised centered range, or whether collisions force parity history.

description: Synthesize sub-million centered signed BY replay scaffold with range-recoverable parity

asi.hypothesis: Centered redundant signed representatives may provide a true sub-million replay body if the sign-conditioned halve can be synthesized and if parity is range-recoverable rather than information-theoretically dirty. The key questions are circuit cost, full 560-step composition, and whether parity cleanup is only a synthesis problem or impossible.
asi.result: Added centered signed circuit helpers and tests. centered_signed_microstep_keeps_narrow_reps_at_submillion_cost synthesizes a sign-conditioned centered signed microstep at 1,560 CCX, replay560≈873,600, peak 1,046q. centered_signed_560_scaffold_hits_submillion_replay_with_live_parity composes all 560 centered signed steps with raw odd/A controls: 873,600 CCX, peak 2,723q, exact tagged-DIV quotient, phase clean, parity history dirty. centered_parity_is_recoverable_from_poststate_range_for_add_cases proves in a small exact centered model that parity is recoverable from poststate+case by testing whether the even preimage is centered: B uses 2*s_out-r_out, A uses r_out-2*s_out, C uses 2*s_out. naive_centered_parity_recovery_cost_would_erase_redundant_replay_win measures naive range recovery at 1,296 CCX/flag, or ≈725,760 CCX for 560 flags. Full BY suite passed locally: 98 tests. Default benchmark path unchanged at 4,111,918 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The centered redundant-signed route is now the best replay body: clean data and phase at 0.874M for 560 steps under the current qubit cap, with no modular reduction flags. Parity is not an information-theoretic blocker in the centered invariant; it is range-recoverable from poststate. But naive post-hoc range recovery is too expensive, so parity cleanup must be folded into inverse/window arithmetic or implemented by a specialized parity-consuming centered halve, not by a separate comparator per bit.
asi.caveat: The 560-step scaffold keeps raw odd/A controls and dirty parity bits; it is not a clean replacement. The range-recovery proof is a small exact model and the naive cleanup cost probe ignores shift Toffoli but includes full add/sub/comparator cost. Integration into point-add and denominator/window generation are still open.
asi.next_action_hint: Prototype a specialized parity-consuming centered halve: use the low bit as a consumed control for ±p correction and shift so parity is encoded into output range without a persistent bit. If that is too hard, try cleaning parity during the inverse centered step where the even preimage arithmetic is already being reconstructed.

description: Extend centered signed BY to inverse replay and sharpen parity cleanup target

asi.hypothesis: For integration, the centered signed replay must work in the inverse/product-clean direction too, and the remaining parity cleanup problem must be narrowed enough to know whether the projected point-add can beat 2.7M. A cheap high-bit range approximation might be sufficient because p/2 is close to 2^255.
asi.result: Added centered_signed_inverse_560_product_clean_scaffold_matches_forward_cost: inverse centered signed replay maps the final centered q frame and zero bottom channel back to the centered q*x product and logical zero at exactly the same 873,600 CCX, peak 2,722q, phase clean. Added centered_signed_replay_budget_hits_google_if_parity_and_denominator_are_folded: scaffold≈285,766 + 2×873,600 centered replays + 2×303,828 selected/tapered denominators ≈2,640,622, below 2.7M if parity cleanup is folded; naive parity cleanup projects ≈4,092,142. Added centered_parity_highbits_recovery_is_too_approximate_without_boundary_fix: high-bit-only range recovery has 65,709/1,120,000 mismatches = 5.87%, above the 1% tolerance. Added highbits_centered_parity_recovery_cost_is_plausible_if_folded: the cheap target would be ≈524 CCX/B-flag, but needs exact boundary correction. Full BY suite passed locally: 102 tests. Default benchmark path unchanged at 4,111,918 / 2716q.
asi.structural_learning: The centered signed route now has both forward and inverse replay bodies at sub-million cost and a whole-point budget under 2.7M if selected denominator and parity folding succeed. The remaining parity problem is not impossibility but exact range-check economics. Pure high bits are too approximate; full range comparators are too expensive. The viable target is a folded or boundary-corrected centered range recovery that reuses inverse/window arithmetic instead of separate post-hoc cleanup.
asi.caveat: Still not a clean integrated point-add path: parity bits remain dirty in the replay scaffolds, and selected denominator generation/application is not wired into mod.rs. The budget assumes parity cleanup is folded into existing arithmetic and denominator windows at low overhead.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement a parity-free centered inverse microstep that reconstructs the even preimage and centers it in-place, using an exact but fused range check. If cost stays far below stored-parity inverse + naive cleanup, adapt the same idea to forward/window replay or use it for pair2 first.

description: Wire centered signed BY replay body into benchmark harness behind env flag
