description: Pivot to BY compact-DIV architecture and test streaming limb selector state

asi.hypothesis: Stop local Kaliski tuning and move to a SOTA-shaped compact DIV: scaled BY replay is already Toffoli-shaped, so the real blocker is branch selector generation without a full denominator pair
asi.result: Added a BY architecture note and a new executable selector model. A streaming limb recurrence generates all sampled 35-window selector sequences exactly with 12 16-bit limbs per A/c entry, but the naive state is 1152 bits; 8 limbs fails on all 64 samples. This is constructive but not low-qubit enough without compressing/recomputing A_j.
asi.next_action_hint: Work on compressing or factoring the streaming A_j selector state from pattern/matrix history; do not spend more effort on threshold micro-optimizations

description: Compress BY streaming selector by folding constant p column

asi.hypothesis: The streaming selector's first matrix column only multiplies the constant p and can be folded into the carry, reducing selector state without changing the BY-DIV architecture
asi.result: Added constant-p folded selector model. It is exact on 64 sampled denominators with 17 limbs per entry and fails at 16 limbs on all samples. State drops from 1152 bits to 1088 bits but remains above the ~600-bit target.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue compressing selector state; next target is factor/recompute the x-column and carry from pattern history rather than carrying four 17-limb entries live

description: Invalidate projective-normalized BY streaming selector compression

asi.hypothesis: Because BY branch choices are invariant under common odd scaling, maybe the folded streaming selector can be projectively normalized to c0=1 and reduced to three 17-limb entries
asi.result: Projective normalized selector would be 816 bits, but it fails on all 64 sampled denominators; normalization discards high 2-adic information needed by later windows. The exact folded state remains 1088 bits.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue simple h/projective normalization. Try Hermite/window factorization or recomputing x-column/carry from compressed pattern history instead.

description: Reduce BY streaming selector model to separate widths and identify 528-bit tail core

asi.hypothesis: The folded streaming selector entries have different precision needs, and after the first 16 windows the quantum x tail is exhausted so only carry state should remain
asi.result: Separate widths reduce exact selector state to 816 bits: two 9-limb x-column entries plus 17/16-limb carry entries. After x tail exhaustion, branch generation uses only a 528-bit carry core; 8-limb x-column and 15-limb c1 variants fail early.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on scheduling/cleanup around the first 16-window x-column workspace: borrow or reverse the 288-bit x-column state, and avoid storing raw pattern history simultaneously

description: Measure first-16 BY pattern history entropy for low-gate selector schedule

asi.hypothesis: If the 288-bit x-column workspace is recomputed/borrowed instead of carried, the only persistent early-selector payload is first-16 pattern history plus the 528-bit carry core
asi.result: First-16 pattern entropy is about 195.4 bits; fixed per-window IDs need 208 bits. Carry core plus fixed early history is 736 bits: plausible for a 1425q low-gate selector schedule, but still above the 663 extra-qubit allowance for 1175q low-qubit.
asi.next_action_hint: Pursue a low-gate exact architecture first: compressed first16 history + 528-bit carry core + borrowed 288-bit b-workspace; low-qubit needs further carry compression or streaming without persistent early history

description: Invalidate rolling in-place use of truncated BY x-column selector state

asi.hypothesis: The 9-limb x-column state in the 816-bit selector might be tempting to carry as a rolling in-place register, but its updates are truncated residues
asi.result: Simple C-branch doubling modulo 2^144 is two-to-one, proving the truncated x-column is not locally reversible as a rolling register. It must be recomputed from retained pattern history, kept exact at a wider width, or cleaned by a new MBUC phase method.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat the 288-bit x-column as borrowed/recomputed scratch, not persistent reversible state; keep focusing on first16-history + 528-bit carry-core schedules

description: Compress post-tail BY selector to a 304-bit ratio state

asi.hypothesis: After the first 16 windows consume all quantum x bits, the remaining BY branches may only need the projective ratio h=g/f instead of two carry rows
asi.result: Validated a 304-bit tail ratio state: after prefix window 16, h=g/f mod 2^304 streams the remaining 304 branch bits exactly on 64 sampled denominators and tapers to zero. Combined with 208 first16 pattern-ID bits, selector payload is ~512 bits in information terms.
asi.next_action_hint: Now focus on a reversible/cheap transition from first16 carry state to tail h and on using the 304-bit h register as consumed history, not on Kaliski micro-optimizations

description: Validate full 560-bit BY ratio selector for all branch history

asi.hypothesis: BY branch decisions depend only on delta and h=g/f, so a full 560-bit h0=x*p^-1 mod 2^560 may replace all denominator-pair, x-column, carry, and pattern-history selector state
asi.result: Validated on 64 sampled denominators: the closed ratio update from h0 streams all 560 branch bits exactly, matches the signed divstep delta trajectory, and tapers to zero. Selector/history state is 560 bits, inside the ~600-bit low-qubit target allowance in information terms.
asi.next_action_hint: Shift architecture work from limb/carry compression to circuitizing full-ratio selector: estimate constant multiply h0 cost and, most importantly, find a reversible low-cost A-step h'=(h-1)/(2h) implementation

description: Identify A-step inverse-density blocker for full BY ratio selector

asi.hypothesis: The full 560-bit ratio selector is state-optimal, but the A update may hide dense modular inversion work
asi.result: Confirmed the A-step obstruction: one toy 16-bit high output bit has ANF degree 14 and density 7268/32768; real 560-step secp256k1 traces average about 132 A-steps and max 149 in 64 samples. Naive per-A inversion is not SOTA-shaped.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for a windowed/Möbius update, inverse-maintenance invariant, or return to larger linear carry states; do not assume full-ratio state alone solves gate cost

description: Probe 16-step Möbius denominator valuation for windowed ratio selector

asi.hypothesis: Windowing the full-ratio update may make the Möbius denominator nearly constant if the h coefficient m01 has high 2-adic valuation
asi.result: 16-step windows always had even m01, but 45.5% of sampled windows had only v2(m01)=1 or 2. Histogram over 2240 windows: [0,347,673,380,317,164,104,55,49,17,18,2,2,9,9,2,92]. A geometric-series inverse gets some help but not a free update.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not rely on simple high-v2 denominator expansion alone; explore inverse-maintenance invariants or accept a larger linear carry selector if gates dominate

description: Estimate full-ratio h0 constant multiply cost as non-blocking

asi.hypothesis: The full-ratio selector's initial h0=x*p^-1 mod 2^560 constant multiply may be affordable relative to the replay budget
asi.result: Naive shifted controlled-add model gives summed add widths 110,720 and compute+uncompute estimates of 221k--443k Toffoli for 1--2 Toffoli/bit adders; p^-1 popcount over 560 bits is 305. This is not the main blocker compared with the A-step inverse problem.
asi.next_action_hint: Prioritize the A-step/windowed inverse circuit; h0 initialization can likely fit inside the projected 1.9M--2.7M architecture budget

description: Check wider ratio windows for Möbius inverse valuation relief

asi.hypothesis: Increasing the ratio window size might raise v2(m01) enough that the window Möbius denominator inverse becomes cheap
asi.result: W=32 still leaves many low-valuation denominators: 822/1088 sampled windows have v2(m01)<=4. Larger windows reduce count but do not make the inverse almost constant; simple window-size increase is not enough.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop relying on geometric-series inversion from denominator valuation alone; investigate inverse-maintenance or linear carry fallback

description: Reject serial per-A inverse implementation for full-ratio selector

asi.hypothesis: A low-scratch bit-serial inverse could implement the full-ratio A-step without the huge ANF/table cost
asi.result: Rejected by budget proxy: summing t^2/2 over actual A-step positions gives mean 7.31M and max 8.89M convolution work units before cleanup or modular replay. This is not SOTA-shaped.
asi.next_action_hint: Full-ratio selector needs a qualitatively better inverse-maintenance trick; otherwise pivot back to linear carry/history selector despite larger state

description: Discover compact BY denominator-pair history sink with 49-bit sidecar

asi.hypothesis: Instead of compressing ratio state, use the shrinking BY denominator pair itself as the entropy sink for raw branch history, with a small sidecar for early slack deficit
asi.result: On 1024 sampled denominators, a 512-bit magnitude pair plus 49-bit sidecar holds raw branch history at every prefix; max convergence step was 558 and worst deficit was exactly 49. Including two sign bits, selector/history is ~563 bits and uses linear divstep updates, avoiding the full-ratio A-step inverse blocker.
asi.next_action_hint: This supersedes full-ratio as the primary SOTA path. Next prove/enforce the 49-bit bound and design a reversible history allocator/compactor in pair high-zero slack, then couple it to scaled BY replay

description: Strengthen compact denominator-pair history sink sampling to 8192 denominators

asi.hypothesis: The denominator-pair slack + 49-bit sidecar bound might hold beyond a small sample
asi.result: Increased the sampled check to 8192 denominators; max convergence remained 558 and worst raw-history deficit remained 49 bits. The compact pair+sidecar route remains the leading selector architecture.
asi.next_action_hint: Attempt a proof/guard for the progress invariant step + bitlen(f)+bitlen(g) <= 561, then design the reversible high-zero history allocator

description: Validate fixed-schedule BY pair-history sidecar at 50 bits

asi.hypothesis: A fixed predeclared high-zero slack schedule may avoid data-dependent history compaction for the compact denominator-pair selector
asi.result: On an independent 8192-sample seed, the per-step worst-bitlength fixed schedule needs a 50-bit sidecar, with worst case at step 560. Adaptive per-trace slack needed 49 bits on the prior seed. Using 50 bits gives selector/history about 564 bits including signs, still below the ~600-bit target.
asi.next_action_hint: Use a 50-bit sidecar in future designs; implement fixed-schedule high-zone allocation first, then consider adaptive compaction only if fixed arithmetic masking is too expensive

description: Hard-gate compact BY pair-history route by invalidating raw per-bit denominator replay

asi.hypothesis: The compact denominator-pair+sidecar BY selector is only worthwhile if its denominator update is not raw per-bit replay
asi.result: Invalidated raw per-bit use immediately: tapered direct denominator compute is 1,004,080 CCX, compute+uncompute is 2,008,160 CCX, and projected total with one replay+scaffold is ~3,793,920 CCX. This is not SOTA. Only selected fixed-matrix/window denominator update remains viable, with an optimistic compute+uncompute model ~607,657 CCX but non-production peak 3424q.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not wire full BY. Only attempt a single-window selected fixed-matrix+history allocator prototype; kill BY entirely if it cannot hit ~10k CCX/window and low-qubit shape.

description: Invalidate remaining selected-window BY route by variable-coefficient lower bound

asi.hypothesis: The only remaining BY escape was selected fixed-matrix windows; if quantum-selected coefficient row formation is cheap enough, BY might still be SOTA-shaped
asi.result: Killed the escape: an optimistic lower-bound circuit for one selected coefficient multiply costs 18,598 CCX; a single selected window lower bound costs 148,784 CCX at 2123q before lookup, signs, q corrections, cleanup, or history allocation. This is >14x the ~10k/window target.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop BY work with current primitives. Pivot to non-BY architecture: investigate Google-style MBUC/kickmix/coset/windowed arithmetic or a new phase-clean IMUL/DIV primitive, validating with isolated lower-bound tests first.

description: Invalidate Montgomery q-history MBUC rescue for in-place multiply in output frame

asi.hypothesis: Montgomery multiplication's internal q-history might be a structured MBUC target for the missing phase-clean in-place multiply primitive
asi.result: In the (x,old_y) frame q-history is sparse-ish (n=8 density 2440/65536), but actual in-place cleanup needs phase correction in the surviving (x,z) output frame. There it becomes dense quotient-like: degree 16/16 and density 31032/65536 for p=251. This kills the q-history MBUC rescue with current primitives.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue Montgomery q-history MBUC unless a phase correction can preserve old_y or exploit extra structure without losing in-place savings; continue searching for a fundamentally different IMUL/DIV primitive or Google-style kickmix pattern

description: Probe Kaliski tagged-DIV coefficient channel as direct division alternative; phase-unsafe

asi.hypothesis: Existing KAL_TAGGED_DIV_COEFF_CHANNEL could delete pair1 numerator multiplications by carrying the numerator through Kaliski as a direct division channel
asi.result: Full harness crashed on alt-seed phase cleanliness: altseed_phase_batches_total=1 with no classical or ancilla mismatches. This direct Kaliski coefficient-channel DIV is phase-unsafe in the exact harness.
asi.rollback_reason: Env-gated probe failed phase cleanliness; no code changes should be kept from this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use the existing coefficient-channel DIV path as a SOTA route unless its MBUC phase source is understood and fixed; prioritize new primitives or Google-style kickmix analysis.

description: Invalidate generic top-level MBUC single-inversion cleanup via dense point-subtraction phase

asi.hypothesis: A single-inversion affine scaffold could compute R out-of-place, X-measure old P, and phase-correct using P=R-Q instead of running a second inverse/uncompute
asi.result: Toy secp256k1-shaped curve kills the cheap version: the phase oracle for a mask of old P bits as a function of surviving R has ANF degree 15/16 and density 19540/65536. Generic top-level MBUC is a dense point-subtraction phase oracle, not a free cleanup.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue generic top-level MBUC for the affine reversibility wall. Only specialized kickmix/MBUC patterns with demonstrably sparse phase correction are worth testing.

description: Reconfirm default exact benchmark after coefficient-channel and MBUC invalidation notes

asi.hypothesis: Default exact path should remain unchanged and clean while the docs record that coefficient-channel tagged DIV is phase-unsafe on retest and top-level MBUC is dense.
asi.result: Full ./autoresearch.sh passes with avg_toffoli=4,089,274, qubits=2713, emitted_ops=30,575,944, correctness_ok=1, and zero alt-seed classical/phase/ancilla batches.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue only with new kickmix/compact arithmetic primitives; do not wire BY or coefficient-channel DIV without a small primitive that is already SOTA-shaped and phase-clean.

description: Close curve-support caveat for generic top-level MBUC with support-restricted degree scaling

asi.hypothesis: The previous full-domain ANF kill for top-level old-point MBUC might be too pessimistic because the output register is curve-supported; test whether a low/constant-degree phase extension exists on curve support only.
asi.result: Support-restricted interpolation still grows with n: min degrees n=4,6,8,10,12 are 1,3,3,4,4. This matches the dimension-threshold extrapolation d≈0.22n≈56 at secp256k1, not a constant-degree phase identity.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use generic top-level MBUC as the single-inversion cleanup. Only pursue specialized kickmix phase corrections if a tiny structured primitive has a sparse/low-degree circuit on support.

description: Invalidate small-state reverse decoding rescue for destructive Montgomery in-place multiply

asi.hypothesis: Although destructive Montgomery multiplication is not locally reversible, the full final product and x uniquely determine y; perhaps reverse-stepping has a small ambiguity frontier usable as a compact cleanup state.
asi.result: Reverse frontier saturates to the field-sized [0,2p) set: n=8 frontier=502, n=10=2042, n=12=8186. Enforcing t0=0 is global backtracking/nonlocal quotient work, not a small reversible cleanup.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revive destructive Montgomery via trellis decoding. Any missing in-place IMUL must avoid consumed-bit ambiguity algebraically rather than decoding it after the fact.

description: Invalidate lambda-from-lambda-square cleanup as dense square-root phase

asi.hypothesis: A one-Kaliski scaffold might preserve old denominator data and uncompute/recover lambda from lambda^2 = Rx + dx + 2Qx using square root instead of a second division.
asi.result: Canonical sqrt phase is dense/high-degree on toy primes: n=8 degree 8 density 126/256, n=10 degree 9 density 502/1024, n=12 degree 12 density 2072/4096. On secp256k1 sqrt is exponentiation, so this is not a low-cost cleanup primitive.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue lambda-square/sqrt cleanup. The remaining Strategy E requirement is still a genuine phase-clean in-place DIV/IMUL, not square-root recovery.

description: Invalidate base-field Montgomery Edwards Hessian model rewrites by torsion obstruction

asi.hypothesis: Maybe secp256k1 can be moved to a cheaper base-field curve model (Montgomery, Edwards, Hessian/twisted-Hessian) with better addition laws and then converted back.
asi.result: Base-field torsion blocks the standard efficient models: secp256k1 prime order has order mod 2=1 and order mod 3=1. Montgomery/Edwards need rational 2-torsion; Hessian-style models need rational 3-torsion; birational maps preserve torsion.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue base-field model rewrites. Projective/isogenous/extension-field variants only matter if they also solve exact affine conversion cleanup with a new primitive.

description: Invalidate sequential one-coordinate old-input MBUC as still growing-degree phase

asi.hypothesis: Top-level MBUC might be rescued by measuring old coordinates sequentially: keep old x live while X-measuring old y, so phase correction depends on (old_x,Rx,Ry) instead of R alone.
asi.result: Support-restricted interpolation still grows: n=4,6,8,10 minimum degrees are 1,2,2,3. Extra side information lowers toy degree but does not reveal a constant-degree identity; dimension extrapolation for 3n variables is still around degree 49 at secp256k1.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue generic sequential old-input MBUC. Any useful MBUC must target specialized arithmetic scratch/carries/lookup garbage, not old affine coordinates.

description: Invalidate one-pair coefficient-transform DIV preserve-x constant-tag rescue

asi.hypothesis: A Kaliski coefficient-transform DIV might use one coefficient pair with r0=constant to preserve x in the lower output while a constant tag in s0=y+beta makes the upper output k*y plus a known contaminant.
asi.result: For T=[[a,k],[x,0]], r0=rho and s0=y+beta gives s=rho*x and r=k*y+(rho*a+beta*k). Sampled (a,k,1) are affine-non-collinear, so no constants rho,beta,C make rho*a+beta*k known across x. One coefficient pair cannot simultaneously preserve x and expose quotient by constant tagging.
asi.next_action_hint: If coefficient-transform DIV is revisited, it needs either a second coefficient channel, a data-dependent cancellation of a(x), or a different triangular Euclidean transform. Constant tags are dead.

description: Invalidate data-dependent a(x) cancellation for one-pair coefficient DIV as dense

asi.hypothesis: The one-pair coefficient-transform DIV might still preserve x and expose y/x if it computes and subtracts the data-dependent contaminant a(x) cheaply.
asi.result: Toy Kaliski a(x) mask phases are full-degree and near-half-density: n=4 density 12/16, n=8 132/256, n=12 2094/4096 with degree n. Data-dependent a(x) cancellation is essentially another branch/inverse computation, not a tiny kickmix phase.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue one-pair coefficient DIV via a(x) cancellation. Only a second coefficient channel, r-as-output design, or a different triangular Euclidean transform remains plausible.

description: Invalidate live post-state branch oracle for forward-only self-cleaning Kaliski on exhaustive toys

asi.hypothesis: Forward-only self-cleaning Kaliski might uncompute branch controls from the live post-state (u,v,r,s,f), avoiding m_hist and a backward denominator replay.
asi.result: Exhaustive tagged toy fields refute live post-state as a branch oracle: even with iteration index and full post-state, conflicts occur (n=4:108, n=5:1200, n=6:5760). The earlier secp sample was only sparse evidence, not an invariant.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not rely on post-state inspection for self-cleaning Kaliski. Need explicit history, a different invariant/transform, pebbling, or a controlled approximate exception argument.

description: Invalidate QROM-selected hybrid Kaliski matrix windows by variable-coefficient lower bound

asi.hypothesis: Hybrid/windowed Kaliski might still be the two-inversion SOTA path if short matrix hints select t-step matrices and delete most cswaps/comparators.
asi.result: Variable-coefficient row-formation lower bound kills generic QROM-selected matrices: t=4/8/16 invocation lower bounds are 4.19M/3.77M/3.63M before QROM, signs, output cleanup, and old-register cleanup. Needed target is ~0.9M per invocation.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue QROM-selected Kaliski matrix windows with current arithmetic. Any windowed-Kaliski revival must avoid quantum variable coefficients entirely, e.g. fixed-control shared algebra rather than selected row coefficients.

description: Invalidate direct initial-x branch-history oracle for self-cleaning Kaliski as dense

asi.hypothesis: Although branch history is deterministic from the preserved initial denominator x, maybe a compressed self-cleaning Kaliski can regenerate branch bits from x using a small direct oracle instead of storing history.
asi.result: Toy branch-history parity as a function of x is high-degree/dense: n=8 degree 8 density 116/256, n=10 degree 10 density 520/1024, n=12 degree 11 density 1976/4096. Direct x-to-history oracle is essentially rerunning Kaliski, not a tiny gadget.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not rely on direct compressed x-to-branch oracles. Kaliski history compression must use explicit encoded history/pebbling or a different transform, not a cheap Boolean shortcut from x.

description: Establish 256-bit information floor for exact Kaliski branch-history compression

asi.hypothesis: Maybe exact Kaliski branch history can be compressed far below raw 407 bits, making branch-stream/pebbled DIV viable without needing a cheap branch oracle.
asi.result: Complete branch sequences are injective in exhaustive toy fields and collision-free on 4096 secp samples, so exact branch-history payload has a ~log2(p)=256-bit floor. This can save at most ~150 bits from raw m_hist, not collapse to ~64-100 bits.
asi.next_action_hint: History-only compression is insufficient for low-qubit SOTA unless combined with major state folding. Focus on different transforms or explicit 256-bit history designs only if their arithmetic also beats current Kaliski.

description: Quantify qubit-floor kill for exact coefficient-transform Kaliski history routes

asi.hypothesis: Combine the 256-bit exact Kaliski history floor with remaining coefficient-transform DIV layouts to see whether exact coefficient-transform Kaliski can still hit Google low-qubit.
asi.result: It cannot hit low-qubit: scratch allowance beyond tx,ty is 663q; r-as-output coefficient DIV floor is 768q and second-channel floor is 1280q before flags, carries, comparators, or add workspace. This kills exact coefficient-transform Kaliski as a 1175q route.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue exact coefficient-transform Kaliski for low-qubit. It could only matter as a low-gate-ish route if a new history/invariant removes the 256-bit floor or if approximate exceptions are allowed and quantified.

description: Invalidate approximate full-poststate self-cleaning Kaliski as non-rare ambiguity

asi.hypothesis: Maybe the full-poststate branch recovery collisions in tagged coefficient-transform Kaliski are rare enough to treat as an approximate exceptional set.
asi.result: Exhaustive toy fields with nonzero tag s0=x+y show ambiguity is common, not rare: ambiguous transition occurrences grow from 19.0% at n=4 to 24.32% at n=8. This invalidates approximate full-poststate self-cleaning as a small-exception route.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue poststate-only Kaliski branch cleanup, exact or approximate. Any Kaliski-derived DIV now needs explicit history, a different invariant, or should be deprioritized in favor of non-Kaliski triangular/MBUC primitives.

description: Prove secp tagged poststates locally admit multiple exact predecessor branches

asi.hypothesis: Maybe secp-sized tagged Kaliski poststates still have a unique locally consistent inverse branch despite toy collisions, allowing an exact or approximate self-cleaning branch predicate.
asi.result: No: enumerating exact inverse candidates on 20 secp trajectories gives ambiguous local predecessor branches for 7205/8140 steps (88.5%). This is a direct local obstruction, not just random collision evidence.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop poststate-only self-cleaning Kaliski. Any Kaliski coefficient DIV must carry explicit history or introduce a new invariant that changes the state transition itself.

description: Invalidate direct lambda-measurement MBUC cleanup as growing-degree division phase

asi.hypothesis: Maybe after computing affine output R, the slope lambda register can be X-measured and phase-corrected cheaply from R and fixed Q, deleting the second inversion.
asi.result: Toy support-restricted interpolation for lambda=-(Ry+Qy)/(Rx-Qx) still has growing minimum degree: n=4,6,8,10,12 -> 2,3,3,4,4. This is a division phase, not a cheap kickmix identity.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue direct lambda MBUC unless a specific sparse phase identity is found. The remaining viable single-inversion paths still require a new in-place DIV/IMUL or fundamentally different coordinate/invariant.

description: Invalidate no-history Kim unconditional Kaliski by local branch ambiguity

asi.hypothesis: Kim 2026 wide unconditional Kaliski might remove the terminal flag and all per-round history by recovering swap/both_odd from the poststate, making a low-qubit inversion rewrite plausible.
asi.result: The wide arithmetic remains algebraically valid, but poststate branch recovery fails: exact local inverse enumeration on reached secp states has ambiguity in 5178/10240 steps (50.6%). Kim removes terminal history, not branch history.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not attempt a no-history Kim drop-in. Only revisit Kim together with explicit history compression/register sharing, or with a new invariant that makes swap/both_odd locally reversible.

description: Invalidate Luo/PZ register-sharing inversion as low-gate SOTA route

asi.hypothesis: Luo/PZ register sharing might be a literature route to Google SOTA if its qubit savings hide a competitive inversion primitive.
asi.result: It is a qubit lever but not low-gate SOTA-shaped: converting the published ~976 n^3 whole-ECDLP slope gives 31,981,568 Toffoli per point-add proxy at n=256, 11.85x/15.23x above Google low-qubit/low-gate point-add budgets before affine cleanup.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue Luo/PZ as a gate route. Only use Luo ideas if qubits become the only binding constraint and a separate low-gate DIV primitive exists.

description: Invalidate slope-pencil coordinate rewrite by dense root-choice phase

asi.hypothesis: Maybe a slope-pencil coordinate through fixed Q can compress affine point-add into a cheap root/sign update, avoiding the second inversion or old-coordinate MBUC.
asi.result: The slope alone is a 2-to-1 coordinate; selecting the correct non-Q intersection is sqrt-like. Toy ANF for a canonical root-choice phase is dense/high-degree: n=12 degree=11, density=2006/4096 over 2083 supported slopes.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue slope-pencil/compressed-coordinate rewrites for this affine benchmark unless a new sparse root-choice identity appears.

description: Show linear x-retagging cannot fix tagged Kaliski poststate ambiguity

asi.hypothesis: Maybe the tagged Kaliski poststate ambiguity is an artifact of the s0=y+x tag and can be fixed with another linear tag s0=y+beta*x while preserving quotient extraction.
asi.result: No: exhaustive toy checks for nonzero beta values show identical ambiguity fractions to beta=1 (e.g. n=8 frac=0.2432). Retagging avoids only zero-scalar exceptions; it does not encode branch history.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop trying constant/linear x-tags for self-cleaning Kaliski. Only non-linear branch-dependent tags could help, which is equivalent to storing/recomputing history.

description: Invalidate raw product scratch MBUC as dense carry phase

asi.hypothesis: Maybe remaining 2n-bit schoolbook product scratch can be measurement-uncomputed cheaply because both inputs x,y are preserved, with phase corrections mask·(x*y) instead of quotient/division phases.
asi.result: Exhaustive ANF shows product high-bit phases are dense carry functions: at n=10 the all-bit mask has degree 19/20 and density 427812/1048576, and a single high-bit mask has degree 19/20 and density 120581/1048576. Raw product-scratch MBUC is not a free in-place IMUL cleanup.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue product-scratch MBUC as an architecture-level SOTA route. Only revisit as a small multiplier-local tradeoff; missing IMUL/DIV still needs a genuinely different phase-clean primitive.

description: Show chord-product batching only moves the second affine inversion

asi.hypothesis: Maybe the two affine denominators dx and Rx-Qx can be Montgomery-batched by using their product, deleting the second inversion in the lambda cleanup path.
asi.result: The chord cubic gives dx*(Rx-Qx)=3Qx^2-2*lambda*Qy, verified on 200 secp samples. But 1/(Rx-Qx)=dx/(dx*(Rx-Qx)); inverting the product merely moves the second inversion and adds at least one variable multiply (149,889 CCX floor).
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue chord-product/Montgomery-trick batching for the two affine denominators. Single-inversion routes still need a true triangular cleanup or in-place DIV/IMUL primitive.

description: Show any x-only additive Kaliski tag is only scalar relabeling

asi.hypothesis: Maybe linear retagging failed but a nonlinear x-only additive tag s0=y+h(x) can inject enough denominator information to make tagged Kaliski poststate branches locally recoverable.
asi.result: No. For fixed x, y+h(x) is a bijective relabeling of the coefficient scalar (except the skipped zero tag). Exhaustive nonlinear toy tags have exactly the same ambiguity fractions, including n=8 frac=0.2432.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not try more x-only additive tags for coefficient-transform self-cleaning. Any useful tag must be branch-dependent/non-bijective, which is equivalent to storing or recomputing branch history.

description: Invalidate carry-save product scratch MBUC as dense redundant-carry phase

asi.hypothesis: Maybe a redundant carry-save product scratch avoids the dense high-bit carry functions that killed raw binary product MBUC, letting IMUL scratch be X-measured with cheap phases.
asi.result: Toy ANF phases remain dense/full-degree even before final carry propagation: n=8 parity of all carry-save wires has degree 16/16 and density 20440/65536; a top-column mask has degree 15/16 and density 3602/65536. The majority-carry network itself is already the hard phase.
asi.validation_note: run_experiment repeatedly refused full autoresearch in this resumed context with the harness context-window warning; metrics use the previously verified clean default plus the targeted carry-save test metrics recorded in src/point_add/venting.rs.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue carry-save product-scratch MBUC/kickmix as the missing in-place IMUL primitive. Focus on non-product/quotient phase-clean triangular transforms or a genuinely different lookup/carry cleanup mechanism.

description: Invalidate slope-carried coordinate retargeting as dense division

asi.hypothesis: Maybe a 2n-bit slope-carried accumulator (x, lambda_Q) can avoid erasing the hard affine slope and thus bypass the second inversion; for windowed point-add the key question is whether the slope tag can retarget cheaply when the selected table point changes.
asi.result: Retargeting from Q to Q' is lambda_Q'=(Q_y-Q'_y+lambda_Q*(x-Q_x))/(x-Q'_x), i.e. a variable division. The new toy probe sees full-domain n=10 ANF degree 19/20 and density 522204/1048576, plus curve-support n=12 min_degree=4. This is not a cheap coordinate channel/kickmix phase.
asi.validation_note: Targeted test cargo test --release slope_carried_coordinate_retargeting_is_dense_division -- --nocapture passed; full autoresearch still cannot be run in this resumed context because run_experiment returns the context-window guard, so baseline metrics are carried forward unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue single slope-tag accumulators for Google's varying table-point/windowed architecture. Only revisit coordinate channels if they carry enough data to retarget to arbitrary Q without a variable division, which likely means projective/multi-tag state rather than 2n affine-sized state.

description: Invalidate branchless Fermat inversion as far above SOTA

asi.hypothesis: Maybe a branchless fixed addition-chain/Fermat inversion avoids all Euclidean branch-history cleanup and can serve as the missing DIV primitive despite more multiplications.
asi.result: No. The measured current modular-square layer is 148342 CCX. Any exponentiation chain reaching an exponent near 2^256 needs at least 255 square/multiply layers, giving an optimistic single-inverse floor of 37,827,210 CCX before scratch/Bennett costs. This is over an order of magnitude above the full point-add budget.
asi.validation_note: Targeted test cargo test --release fermat_fixed_chain_inversion_floor_misses_sota_by_order -- --nocapture passed and emitted fermat_inv_square_floor_ccx=148342, fermat_inv_chain_floor_ccx=37827210.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue Fermat/addition-chain inversion or fixed-sequence exponentiation as a SOTA route with current multiplication primitives. The only plausible inversion route remains a Euclidean/divstep transform with a new cleanup/history mechanism.

description: Show 600-scratch Kaliski DIV cannot be current one-pair plus small sidecar

asi.hypothesis: With only 600 scratch over the input point registers, maybe a folded Kaliski DIV can use tx as v and ty as the data coefficient s, spend 512 scratch on u+r, and use the remaining 88 qubits as a small sidecar tag to recover branches locally instead of storing m_hist.
asi.result: Exhaustive toy tests give the sidecar every advantage by evolving an independent known coefficient column and revealing its low bits. Exact local branch recovery still needs n-1 sidecar bits: n=4..8 require 3,4,5,6,7 bits. At n=8, a 4-bit sidecar leaves 9500 conflicts. Extrapolated secp256k1 need is about 255 bits, far beyond the 88-bit slack in a 600-scratch one-pair Kaliski layout.
asi.validation_note: Targeted test cargo test --release scratch600_sidecar_tag_bits_do_not_fix_kaliski_branch_recovery -- --nocapture passed and emitted the scratch600 sidecar metrics.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue current Kaliski one-coefficient-pair plus a small tag/sidecar under the 600q scratch cap. A viable DIV must have a locally reversible update convention without field-sized side state, or be a non-Kaliski transform.

description: Show unreduced Kaliski coefficients self-clean only by exceeding 600 scratch

asi.hypothesis: Maybe the branch history obstruction is an artifact of modularly reducing Kaliski coefficient registers; if unreduced coefficients self-clean, that identifies a possible 600-scratch route or at least where the missing information lives.
asi.result: Unreduced coefficient registers do make poststate branch images disjoint on exhaustive toys: zero conflicts through n=8. But coefficient width grows as 3n-1 in the toy schedule; n=8 reaches 23 bits. For secp256k1 this implies about 663-bit coefficients and a folded scratch floor around 1326 qubits (u=256, wide r=663, extension for input s=407), far above the 600-scratch cap.
asi.next_action_hint: The missing primitive can be phrased sharply: get the local reversibility of unreduced coefficients without carrying their high quotient bits. Plain unreduced Kaliski and reduced Kaliski plus small sidecar are both outside 600 scratch.

description: Invalidate MBUC of unreduced Kaliski quotient bits as dense phase

asi.hypothesis: Maybe unreduced Kaliski coefficients can be used only transiently for local reversibility, then their high quotient bits can be X-measured and MBUC-corrected cheaply to return to a 600-scratch layout.
asi.result: No. A representative high quotient bit of the unreduced coefficient is already dense as a phase function. On toy n=10 it has ANF degree 20/20 and density 516314/1048576 even in the generous input frame (x,y). Measuring the quotient bits just turns the Euclidean history into a dense phase oracle.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue MBUC of unreduced Kaliski high bits as a scratch reducer. The route needs a transform that never creates field-sized quotient bits, not one that creates and measures them.

description: Show curve support is not a free Kaliski branch oracle under 600 scratch

asi.hypothesis: Maybe the curve-supported point-add input set makes tagged Kaliski poststate branch recovery rare enough for an approximate no-history 600-scratch DIV; test both global curve-support collisions and purely local secp predecessor candidates.
asi.finding: Toy curve support makes global poststate collisions rare (n14 ambiguous occurrence fraction 0.004763, exact sidecar bits 6), but a local reverse step on actual secp curve samples still sees 0.8836 ambiguous predecessor-candidate fraction from (u,v,r,s,f) alone.
asi.implication: Curve support is only useful if a cheap transformed curve-support predicate distinguishes false predecessors. The obvious per-step shifted curve equation is a cubic field check and not SOTA-shaped.
asi.next_action_hint: Search for a low-cost invariant carrying curve support through the Euclidean coefficient transform; otherwise return to non-Kaliski/local-reversible DIV conventions.

description: Bound simple curve-support 2-adic sidecar above folded Kaliski slack

asi.hypothesis: A curve-supported Kaliski sidecar might fit in the 88q slack if only low 2-adic bits of an independent coefficient column are kept and updated locally.
asi.finding: Best tested implementable two-lane mod-2^b sidecar needs lane bits 2,3,5,6,6 for toy n=8,10,12,14,16; n16 pair bits=12, linear secp extrapolated pair bits=192 vs 88 slack.
asi.implication: Curve support helps but the simple two-lane 2-adic coefficient tag is not a 600-scratch folded-Kaliski solution unless the growth becomes strongly sublinear or can be entropy-coded.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for a genuinely one-lane/closed sidecar, entropy-coded branch rank, or abandon Kaliski sidecar in favor of a different local-reversible DIV convention.

description: Close one-lane linear compression of curve-supported Kaliski sidecar

asi.hypothesis: Maybe the two-lane curve-supported 2-adic sidecar can be compressed to one b-bit linear sidecar with branch-dependent scalar updates.
asi.finding: No nonzero linear functional h=alpha*r+beta*s is closed under all four reachable Kaliski branch matrices modulo 2^1 through 2^8; common eigenvector count mod256 is 0.
asi.implication: The natural one-lane linear/eigenvector route cannot reduce the implementable sidecar pair below the 88q slack. Any compression must be nonlinear finite-state/entropy-coded or use a different invariant.
asi.next_action_hint: Test entropy/rank-coded sidecar feasibility or pivot to non-Kaliski DIV conventions.

description: Show sparse curve-collision rank needs dense event detector

asi.hypothesis: Curve-supported Kaliski collisions are globally sparse, so perhaps an entropy/rank sidecar only needs to store one rare collision bit.
asi.finding: The natural event predicate 'this (dx,dy) trajectory hits a curve-supported ambiguous poststate' is dense in the full input frame: toy n=10 degree 19/20, density 487716/1048576, with 87 ambiguous poststate keys.
asi.implication: Sparse rank coding is not automatically a cheap circuit; it still needs a dense event detector unless a new curve-support-only invariant/extension is found.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue naive sparse collision-rank Kaliski sidecars; look for a different locally reversible DIV convention or a genuinely cheap transformed curve invariant.

description: Invalidate redundant centered Kaliski coefficients as still width-growing

asi.hypothesis: Maybe keeping centered redundant coefficient representatives in a moderately wider range preserves enough quotient history while staying within 600 scratch.
asi.finding: On curve support, exact branch recovery needs extra range bits 6,9,11 for toy n=8,10,12; n=14 still has 301 conflicts at extra=11. Linear extrapolated extra bits for secp are ~235 per lane.
asi.implication: Redundant centered coefficients are just a softened version of unreduced quotient history and do not provide a small fixed-width local-reversibility primitive.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop Kaliski sidecar/range variants unless a new nonlinear invariant appears; pivot to non-Kaliski DIV or arithmetic-state kickmix.

description: Find gate-shaped controlled qoffset via mask and borrowed offset workspace

asi.hypothesis: A controlled quantum-offset add can avoid controlling every dirty-adder primitive by materializing ctrl&offset into a clean mask while borrowing the original offset register as dirty workspace.
asi.finding: Small basis/phase check passes. n=256 masked-borrow qoffset costs 1274 CCX at peak 771q and projects a 560-step replay at 1,142,400 CCX, but compressed history + decoder + mask is ~766 scratch.
asi.implication: The gate target for BY dirty controlled add is reachable, but the clean mask misses the user's 600-scratch cap by ~166q. The remaining issue is mask streaming/overlap, not raw controlled-adder Toffoli.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate whether the 256-bit mask can be windowed/streamed through 16-step BY pattern windows or overlapped with branch-history storage without phase garbage.

description: Model partial qoffset mask tradeoff as just short of 600q low-qubit target

asi.hypothesis: Maybe storing only part of the clean ctrl&offset mask gives a scratch/gate tradeoff that fits 600q and still reaches 2.7M.
asi.finding: Under compressed history (481q) and decoder (26q), only 90 mask bits remain. Linear interpolation between no-mask 3557 CCX and full-mask 1274 CCX gives add≈2755 CCX and projected point-add≈2,764,476 with branch/decode margin.
asi.implication: Partial mask must beat linear interpolation to hit the 600q low-qubit target; the measured full-mask primitive is useful, but naive mask budgeting is just short.
asi.next_action_hint: Try a real 16-bit/window mask streaming construction whose carry interaction may beat the linear model, or abandon BY under strict 600 scratch.

description: Show one-bit streamed qoffset mask fits scratch but misses gate target

asi.hypothesis: A one-bit streamed ctrl&offset mask might fit 600 scratch while retaining the gate savings of the full 256-bit mask.
asi.finding: The streamed-mask qoffset add is phase-clean and scratch-good (scratch_with_history≈510q), but repeated mask compute/uncompute costs 2796 CCX; projected 560-step replay is 1,994,720 CCX.
asi.implication: Naive one-bit mask streaming fits the scratch cap but misses the BY gate target. Mask/carry sharing must beat this linear pattern to be useful.
asi.next_action_hint: If continuing BY, search for a carry-save/windowed controlled-add that computes ctrl&offset once per window or reuses branch history as mask; otherwise pivot away from BY under strict 600 scratch.

description: Optimize streamed qoffset mask enough to hit 600-scratch BY low-qubit target

asi.hypothesis: Simple offset broadcast uses in the streamed-mask dirty qoffset adder do not need a materialized ctrl&offset mask; direct controlled toggles may save enough gates while preserving one-mask-qubit scratch.
asi.finding: The optimized streamed-mask qoffset add is phase-clean, scratch-good, and drops from 2796 to 2542 CCX. With scaled microstep overhead, div560≈1,852,480 and projected point-add≈2,645,196 under the 481-bit compressed-pattern + 26-bit decoder + 3-clean scratch model.
asi.implication: The controlled-add scratch/gate blocker for a 600-scratch BY replay is no longer fatal on paper; this primitive beats the Google 2.7M low-qubit Toffoli target if branch-pattern history compression/cleanup and integration can be made real.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on reversible compressed pattern-history production/cleanup and denominator-derived pattern generation under folded tx/ty. Do not spend time on generic controlled-adder wrappers unless they preserve the one-mask-qubit scratch model.

description: Guardrail: streamed-qoffset BY replay still needs sub-88k selector before integration

asi.hypothesis: The scratch-good streamed qoffset replay should not be integrated unless the selector/pattern source also fits the remaining low-qubit Toffoli margin.
asi.finding: After the measured pattern decoder, the 150k branch/decode allowance leaves only 87,840 CCX for denominator-derived pattern generation. The cheap lowword pattern oracle alone is 208,320 CCX for one denominator and projects to 2,765,676; the exact tapered selector projects to 4,565,516.
asi.implication: This invalidates an immediate BY hook-up despite the positive qoffset primitive. The missing piece is a sub-88k selector source or algebraic folding of selector generation into other work.
asi.rollback_reason: No rollback; this is a guardrail test/doc update preserving default exact metrics.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not wire BY replay into affine path. Either find a selector-generation primitive below ~88k first, or pivot away from BY to another SOTA-shaped DIV cleanup route.

description: Test secp256k1 j=0 endomorphism slope swap and kill it as cleanup primitive

asi.hypothesis: The secp256k1 j=0 automorphism might expose a special slope denominator swap that avoids the usual dx division or gives a cheap lambda cleanup relation.
asi.finding: The identity is real: lambda=(x^2+Qx*x+Qx^2)/(y+Qy) passed 200/200 secp samples with zero y-sum exceptions. But output-frame cleanup using lambda=(Rx^2+Qx*Rx+Qx^2)/(Qy-Ry) is still dense: toy n=10 ANF degree 20/20, density 297278/1048576.
asi.implication: The automorphism is useful algebraic ammunition and may change numerator scheduling, but it is not a standalone one-inversion/IMUL primitive. Do not wire an endomorphism point-add variant unless a DIV/lambda-clean primitive is solved first.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue early-invalidation of non-BY primitives. Possible next checks: whether the x-only quadratic numerator can lower a tagged-DIV coefficient transform in isolation, or pivot to another DIV cleanup invariant.

description: Close curve-support caveat for j=0 endomorphism slope cleanup

asi.hypothesis: The dense full-domain endomorphism slope cleanup phase might be an artifact; restricting interpolation to valid j=0 curve outputs could reveal a constant-degree automorphism identity.
asi.finding: Support-restricted interpolation on j=0 toy primes gives min degrees 1,3,3,3,4 for n=4,6,8,10,12; n12=4. This mirrors the ordinary lambda cleanup support-degree behavior and does not show a constant-degree shortcut.
asi.implication: The secp256k1 automorphism denominator swap remains algebraically useful but not a cleanup primitive. The curve-support caveat for the dense endomorphism phase is closed enough to avoid integration work.
asi.next_action_hint: Move on from endomorphism-based lambda cleanup unless combined with a genuinely new DIV/IMUL primitive. Continue isolated tests for non-BY local reversibility.

description: Invalidate rolling-hash Kaliski sidecar: tiny collision-free history but dense branch-pop decoder

asi.hypothesis: A reversible rolling hash of Kaliski branch history might fit the scratch sidecar and allow reverse branch recovery from poststate plus hash; test whether collision-free toy state implies cheap decoder.
asi.result: Curve-supported toys need only a few hash bits for disambiguation, but the branch-pop decoder over (u,v,r,s,f,h) is high-degree/dense even at n=4 (add degree 18/18, density 27136/262144).
asi.next_action_hint: Only revisit compressed Kaliski history if the sidecar update is co-designed to yield an algebraically sparse decoder; generic entropy/hash sidecars are not enough.

description: Probe ordinary Euclidean quotient-stream DIV; algebra works but packed history misses scratch

asi.hypothesis: Ordinary Euclidean quotient matrices may give a clean non-Kaliski DIV: generate quotient stream from x, replay it on (0,y) to get (y/x,0), and uncompute denominator.
asi.result: Algebra works on 200 secp-sized samples and cost proxy is plausible, but variable quotient history packing is the blocker: raw payload p99 349/max 360 means 256+history reaches 616q, and an unrealistically cheap one-boundary-bit model is 776q p99.
asi.next_action_hint: Only revive this route with a concrete reversible fixed-layout/prefix-stack quotient-packing primitive that overlaps history with data/workspace; otherwise it is another hidden history channel.

description: Close smart prefix-code objection for Euclidean quotient DIV history

asi.hypothesis: Maybe Euclidean quotient-stream DIV can be rescued by smart prefix/arithmetic coding rather than raw quotient payload or one-boundary-bit packing.
asi.result: Empirical ideal code length for secp-sized Euclidean quotient sequences is ~513 bits mean and 518.44 bits p99, so coefficient replay scratch is ~774.44q before decoder/pointer/worst-case costs.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue self-contained Euclidean quotient history compression under 600q; only side-information decoders using live x without extra denominator registers could revive it.

description: Kill generic MBUC cleanup for Euclidean quotient-stream history

asi.hypothesis: Maybe Euclidean quotient history can be X-measured and cleaned by a cheap phase oracle instead of reversibly uncomputed or packed.
asi.result: Representative quotient payload parity phase is dense/high-degree on toy fields, reaching degree 11/12 and density 2046/4096 at n=12.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use generic MBUC/kickmix for Euclidean quotient history; only structured live-x recomputation or a true local DIV primitive remains.

description: Kill naive live-x recomputation parser for Euclidean quotient DIV

asi.hypothesis: Maybe Euclidean quotient replay can avoid self-contained history by recomputing each quotient prefix from the live denominator x on demand and uncomputing it.
asi.result: Naive live-x prefix recomputation has optimistic long-division weight mean 8,746,311 and p99 10,391,412 before real comparator/add constants, far beyond a one-inversion budget.
asi.next_action_hint: Only a radically more direct live-x quotient parser (not prefix recomputation) could revive ordinary Euclidean quotient-stream DIV.

description: Invalidate 2-adic/Hensel inverse as local secp pseudo-Mersenne DIV

asi.hypothesis: Pseudo-Mersenne secp p=2^256-c might allow cheap field inversion by computing a 2-adic/Hensel inverse modulo 2^n and applying a small correction.
asi.result: The correction is itself a full field inverse of d=1+c*t; toy correction phase grows dense/high-degree (n12 degree 11, density 2004/4096), so the hard inverse is only moved.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue 2-adic/Hensel inverse unless a separate cheap correction-denominator inverse appears.

description: Close curve-x-support reciprocal shortcut for inverse garbage cleanup

asi.hypothesis: Maybe denominators are easier than arbitrary field elements because Px-Qx is restricted to the x-coordinate support of the curve.
asi.result: Exhaustive toy interpolation of reciprocal on the curve x-set still needs growing degree: n=8,10,12,14 require degrees 4,5,6,7.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not rely on curve x-support alone for inverse/quotient MBUC or branch cleanup; a candidate must use more structure than just denominator support.

description: Invalidate curve-membership-as-free-branch-oracle via dense Legendre ANF

asi.hypothesis: Curve-support branch recovery might be cheap if the circuit only has to test whether a candidate x lies on y^2=x^3+7.
asi.result: The curve x-membership/Legendre predicate itself has maximal-degree dense toy ANF: n14 degree 14, density 8190/16384.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not treat curve support as a free local discriminator; any branch decoder using it must budget a dense Legendre-style oracle.

description: Show generic in-place variable multiply cleanup is dense division

asi.hypothesis: A schoolbook-cost in-place variable multiply might be made by measuring the old multiplier after keeping the product.
asi.result: The required phase correction is old_b=t/a, an ordinary modular division; toy ANF n10 is degree 18/20 and density 521064/1048576.
asi.next_action_hint: Strategy E still needs a genuine quotient cleanup primitive; do not count MBUC of the old multiplier as cheap.

description: Probe half-GCD matrix checkpoint; compact first matrix but tail/state exceeds scratch

asi.hypothesis: Recursive half-GCD might compress Euclidean quotient history by storing a 2x2 midpoint matrix instead of all quotients.
asi.result: The first matrix checkpoint is compact (p99 524 bits), but matrix+residual is 769 bits and matrix+raw tail payload is 689 bits before parser/cleanup.
asi.next_action_hint: Only revisit half-GCD if matrix application and residual/tail cleanup can be fused so the matrix and tail state are not simultaneously live.

description: Kill MBUC escape for half-GCD checkpoint matrices

asi.hypothesis: Maybe the compact first half-GCD matrix can be X-measured and phase-corrected instead of being stored with the residual tail.
asi.result: A representative checkpoint-matrix parity is dense/high-degree on toy fields: n14 degree 13/14, density 8162/16384.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use generic MBUC for half-GCD matrices; only a fused reversible matrix/tail schedule could revive recursive GCD.

description: Check endomorphism y-denominator support; inverse still grows

asi.hypothesis: The secp256k1 j=0 endomorphism identity may help because it swaps the hard x-Qx denominator to a y+Qy denominator on a smaller y-coordinate support.
asi.result: Toy p=1 mod 3 y-support reciprocal still needs growing interpolation degree: n=10,12,14 -> 4,5,6; not a constant/local cleanup.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the endomorphism identity only as algebraic ammunition; do not use y-denominator support alone as a DIV primitive.

description: Find centered Euclid quotient raw payload fits 600, but parser entropy kills it

asi.hypothesis: Nearest signed Euclidean quotients may reduce quotient-stream payload enough for the tx,ty+600 scratch target.
asi.result: Raw magnitude payload is indeed tantalizing (p99 336, 256+raw=592), but any self-contained parser kills it: boundary scratch p99 710 and entropy scratch p99 702.493.
asi.next_action_hint: Only revisit centered Euclid if a concrete reversible live-state parser consumes raw magnitude bits without boundary history or extra denominator work registers.

description: Kill naive live-x parser for promising centered Euclid raw quotient stream

asi.hypothesis: The promising centered Euclid raw quotient stream might be parsed by recomputing prefixes from live x instead of carrying boundary bits.
asi.result: Naive live-x prefix recomputation is still gate-dead even with centered quotients: optimistic weight mean 5.87M, p99 6.82M for one DIV.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue simple recompute parsers; a revival needs a genuinely streaming reversible parser with no prefix recompute and no boundary bank.

description: Close MBUC escape for centered Euclid raw quotient stream

asi.hypothesis: Centered Euclid raw quotient payload fits 600 scratch only if the stream can be removed or parsed without boundaries; test measurement-based removal of the quotient payload.
asi.result: A representative centered quotient-magnitude parity has maximal degree on toy fields (n14 degree 14, density 8320/16384), so MBUC of the stream is dense just like ordinary quotient streams.
asi.next_action_hint: Centered quotient stream remains alive only with a concrete reversible streaming consumer; do not rely on measurement cleanup.

description: Invalidate plus-minus odd-GCD shift stream as exact 600-scratch DIV history

asi.hypothesis: A plus-minus odd-GCD DIV could beat Euclidean quotient histories by storing only shift counts k from steps {v,(u-v)/2^k}.
asi.result: The k-only stream is tantalizing (p99 shift scratch 564) but not reversible: k-only reverse is ambiguous on 32.3% of toy n12 reached steps, and adding only the needed direction bit gives p99 scratch 752 before delimiters/cleanup.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue plus-minus/shift-count DIV unless a new invariant makes the direction bit locally recoverable.

description: Probe sparse chunked Kaliski prescaler; local win blocked by phase-unsafe long shift

asi.hypothesis: Use secp256k1 sparsity of 2^iters mod p to jump through zero runs in the Kaliski denominator prescaler, making scale absorption cheaper than the deleted correction loops.
asi.result: Local cost probe is promising: chunked prescaler 47,276 CCX vs mixed 106,136/104,606; compute+uncompute would save 8,468/7,703 CCX for pair1/pair2 locally.
asi.blocker: Full folded pair1 harness with chunked shifter had altseed_phase_batches_total=1, also with LOWQ_SHIFT22=1. The current mod_shift_left_by_k is not a phase-clean generic prescaler in this context.
asi.next_action_hint: Only revive scale absorption with a new exact long-shift primitive; do not wire the current chunked shifter into production.

description: Refine plus-minus shift-stream blocker: direction compresses, parser still kills

asi.hypothesis: The earlier plus-minus shift-count stream may have been overcharged by one direction bit per step; test whether the whole k-sequence almost determines directions.
asi.result: Direction entropy is tiny: exhaustive n16 needs max 2 sidecar bits and p99 1. The blocker moves to parsing k values: boundaries scratch p99 759, unary scratch 640, empirical k-symbol entropy scratch 640.213.
asi.next_action_hint: If revisiting plus-minus, focus on a live raw-k parser; do not spend time on direction sidecar compression.

description: Kill raw-concatenated plus-minus k stream via dense rank decoder

asi.hypothesis: Raw-concatenated plus-minus k bits plus a tiny rank sidecar may fit scratch if the valid-parse rank decoder is local.
asi.result: Rank sidecar is information-small but decoder-dense: rank bit ANF reaches degree 14 and density 8254/16384 at toy n14, with max multiplicity only 21.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat plus-minus raw-k compression like rolling-hash Kaliski: compressed history exists, but branch-pop/rank decoding is dense unless a new local parser is found.

description: Kill raw-concatenated centered Euclid quotient stream via dense rank decoder

asi.hypothesis: Raw-concatenated centered Euclid quotient magnitudes plus a tiny rank among valid parses may avoid explicit boundary bits under 600 scratch.
asi.result: The sidecar is information-small (toy n14 max multiplicity 4) but the rank decoder is dense/high-degree: degree 13, density 7838/16384. This kills the smart-boundary objection for centered Euclid.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revisit centered Euclid boundary compression unless a local streaming parser is specified; valid-parse rank decoding is not cheap.

description: Kill naive live-x parser for plus-minus raw k stream

asi.hypothesis: Maybe the plus-minus raw k stream can be parsed by recomputing odd-GCD prefixes from the live denominator instead of carrying boundary/rank parser state.
asi.result: Naive live-x prefix recomputation is gate-dead: optimistic bitlength-width mean 9.30M, p99 10.31M for one DIV.
asi.next_action_hint: Only revive plus-minus raw k with a genuinely streaming local parser; simple recompute, boundary, entropy-code, and rank-decode paths are closed.

description: Invalidate curve-point live reciprocal cleanup as support interpolation

asi.hypothesis: Maybe reciprocal/inverse garbage can be phase-cleaned cheaply from the full live curve point (x,y), even though x-only support interpolation was high degree.
asi.result: Toy interpolation with both x and y live lowers the n14 minimum degree from 7 to 4 but still tracks sparse-support dimension growth; it is support interpolation, not a local cleanup primitive.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not count live y as a free reciprocal cleanup oracle; any real use still needs a low-cost high-dimensional support interpolant at n=256.

description: Find transition-oriented Kaliski swap upper bound

asi.hypothesis: Consecutive Kaliski pre/post cswaps might be batched by carrying a logical orientation across iterations instead of restoring canonical order every step.
asi.result: Classical replay verifies a transition-oriented Kaliski skeleton exactly and gives a cswap word-event ratio about 0.277 versus canonical pre+post swaps. This is an upper bound; orientation-aware predicate/comparator cost remains uncharged.
asi.next_action_hint: Next isolate cost of computing branch controls in a possibly swapped frame (u0/v0/u>v with equality handling) before any production Kaliski rewrite.

description: Kill transition-oriented Kaliski after selector floor

asi.hypothesis: The transition-oriented Kaliski swap reduction might still be useful if orientation-aware branch predicates are cheap.
asi.result: A lower bound of one extra selected-zero OR chain plus one equality/neq chain costs 510 CCX/iter, exceeding the 333-336 CCX/iter cswap savings and projecting +282k Toffoli for point-add before bookkeeping.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue transition-oriented Kaliski unless a comparator/zero-test simultaneously yields both orientations essentially free.

description: Open direct Solinas chunk multihalve path for correction loops

asi.hypothesis: Maybe Kaliski correction loops can be replaced by direct Solinas multi-bit halvings: compute t from low bits so x+t*p is divisible by 2^k, then clear t from the output quotient.
asi.result: Numeric k=22 direct multihalve matches repeated halving, chains to 404 halvings, and output quotient cleanup is sparse on toys (n14 degree 13 but density only 16/16384).
asi.next_action_hint: Implement an isolated phase-clean in-place chunk-halving circuit/cost model; key tasks are reversible t computation, t*c product scratch cleanup, and output threshold correction.

description: Cost direct Solinas multihalve correction-loop chunk

asi.hypothesis: Direct Solinas multihalve might materially reduce the Kaliski correction-halving loops if its quotient cleanup is sparse.
asi.result: First scratch/product design beats repeated halving but only modestly: 22-halving chunk 5610→4910 CCX floor, 8-halving chunk 2040→1724, projected 404-halving saving 12.9k before real threshold adjustment.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not production-wire yet; either tighten product/threshold implementation or use this primitive only if it also repairs the phase-unsafe chunked prescaler path.

description: Tighten Solinas multihalve cost using split pseudo-Mersenne constant

asi.hypothesis: The direct Solinas multihalve product cost can be reduced by splitting c=2^32+977 instead of materializing full t*c.
asi.result: Split product reduces the optimistic k=22 floor from 4910 to 3858 CCX and k=8 from 1724 to 1344, raising projected 404-halving-loop saving from 12.9k to 32.2k CCX.
asi.next_action_hint: Next blocker is exact threshold-correction circuit and phase validation; if clean, this is a modest ~64k two-loop point-add saving and may be useful for prescaler repair.

description: Quantify approximate Kaliski iteration trimming ceiling

asi.hypothesis: Google-style approximate correctness might make simple Kaliski iteration trimming a SOTA-scale explanation.
asi.result: On 4096 curve-pair samples, p99 terminal iterations were only 385 for both denominators and p999 was 391/392; trimming to 392 gives ~0.061% per-denominator failure, but saves only a dozen-ish iterations versus exact 404/401, far below the >1M Toffoli gap.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat approximate iteration trimming as small final tuning only; keep focus on BY selector or new reversible DIV/IMUL cleanup structure.

description: Cost exact Solinas multihalve cleanup and open history-carry chunk route

asi.hypothesis: Direct Solinas multihalve cleanup might become exact cheaply; if threshold recomputation is too expensive, keeping the one-bit residual per chunk through the body may avoid dense cleanup.
asi.result: Concrete threshold predicate costs kill the clean no-history version (best exact DP for 404 costs 107296 vs current 103020), but history-carry chunks are exact in numeric forward/reverse tests and project 240540 CCX roundtrip saving over pair1+pair2 for 115 residual bits.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not wire threshold-recompute multihalve. Next isolated step is a real phase-clean long-shift/history-carry circuit skeleton with explicit inverse and peak accounting; only then consider replacing correction loops or repairing chunked prescaler.

description: Baseline scratch-qubit accounting after pivot to low-scratch architectures

asi.hypothesis: Reframe optimization around the hard low-scratch target rather than local Toffoli improvements; current exact circuit scratch is total qubits minus four 256-bit I/O registers.
asi.result: Default clean baseline uses 2713 total qubits, i.e. about 1689 scratch/ancilla over the 1024 input/output/register footprint; this exceeds a 600-scratch target by about 1089 qubits.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop treating Kaliski polishing as the main path. Build and compare only architectures whose live state can plausibly fit tx,ty plus ~600 scratch, then estimate Toffoli honestly including selector/history/cleanup.

name: quantum_ecc secp256k1 point-add scratch beyond tx_ty

description: Correct scratch baseline as qubits beyond tx,ty only

asi.hypothesis: The low-qubit target should count scratch beyond the two quantum point registers tx,ty, not beyond classical offset registers.
asi.result: Current clean circuit uses 2713 quantum qubits; subtracting tx,ty=512 leaves 2201 scratch/ancilla qubits, about 1601 over the ~600 scratch target.
asi.next_action_hint: Prioritize architectures with persistent live state <=~600 beyond tx,ty. Kaliski layouts with u,v,r,s/history are excluded except as contrast/baseline.

description: Attempt targeted scratch600 frontier test but harness requires autoresearch.sh

asi.hypothesis: Run isolated scratch600 frontier test directly to log low-scratch architecture metrics.
asi.rollback_reason: run_experiment rejected custom cargo test because autoresearch.sh exists and must be used for experiments.
asi.next_action_hint: Use autoresearch.sh with an optional extra-test hook or run full autoresearch validation after adding the frontier test.

description: Scratch600 frontier table first attempt underflowed on uncharged selector row

asi.hypothesis: Executable low-scratch frontier table should identify the best fully charged <=600-scratch candidate.
asi.error_details: Test accidentally treated streamed-mask replay-body-only row as fully charged, so 2_645_196 - 2_700_000 underflowed usize and assertion failed.
asi.rollback_reason: Fix the candidate classification to exclude uncharged selector/parser rows from fully charged SOTA comparison.
asi.next_action_hint: Add a charged_selector flag or set charged_toffoli=None for replay-body-only rows, rerun through autoresearch.sh extra-test hook.

description: Rank scratch-600 architectures and charge selector/parser blockers

asi.hypothesis: After pivoting away from Kaliski, rank only architectures whose persistent state can fit tx,ty plus <=600 scratch and charge known selector/parser/cleanup costs.
asi.result: Several state shapes fit <=600 scratch, but none is fully solved. The best charged near-miss is streamed-mask BY with scratch≈510 and projected 2,765,676 Toffoli when the lowword selector is included, 65,676 above the 2.7M target; the uncharged replay-body projection is 2,645,196 and leaves only 54,804 CCX for all missing selector work. Centered Euclid, plus-minus, and half-GCD fit only before parser/tail costs are charged.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on the streamed-mask BY selector shortfall or a fused half-GCD/parser-free DIV. Do not spend main-loop time on Kaliski state reductions unless they produce <=600-scratch full architecture.

description: Baseline projected Toffoli gap for best charged scratch600 candidate

asi.hypothesis: Use the fully charged Toffoli gap of the best <=600-scratch candidate as the active optimization metric.
asi.result: Baseline low-scratch frontier is the streamed-mask BY route with lowword selector charged: 510 scratch bits, projected 2,765,676 Toffoli, gap 65,676 above the 2.7M target.
asi.next_action_hint: Try to reduce selector production below the lowword-oracle 208,320 CCX, or find another <=600-scratch candidate with lower charged Toffoli.

description: Find cheaper w4 lowword selector oracle under scratch600 BY model

asi.hypothesis: The old 16-step lowword BY selector may be too coarse; smaller subwindows could reduce reversible lowword simulation and delta-decoder cost enough to close the 65k projected gap under the same selector-oracle model.
asi.result: w=4 lowword oracle+decoder totals 189,840 CCX for 560 steps versus 270,480 for w=16, improving the streamed-mask BY selector-only projection from 2,765,676 to 2,685,036 (-14,964 vs 2.7M). Full denominator/selector state plumbing between lowword windows is still uncharged, so this is not a production claim.
asi.next_action_hint: Charge the missing w=4 denominator-state update/cleanup. Try a 4-step ratio/carry update or a compact pair+sidecar schedule; discard if plumbing exceeds ~15k CCX or pushes scratch above 600.

name: scratch600 BY w4 missing denominator plumbing excess

description: Charge naive full-pair plumbing for w4 lowword selector

asi.hypothesis: The w4 lowword selector projection only helps if the missing denominator/selector state update is nearly free; measure the obvious exact full-pair update to establish the plumbing floor.
asi.result: Naive full-pair 4-step denominator update costs 6,564 CCX/window, 918,960 CCX for 560-step compute and 1,837,920 for compute+uncompute, exceeding the w4 selector slack by 903,996 CCX. Peak 1,104q also exceeds scratch600 if counted as extra state. This kills direct full-pair plumbing for the promising w4 selector oracle.
asi.next_action_hint: Try compact ratio/carry or fixed-matrix update for w4; it must beat the direct full-pair compute by roughly two orders of magnitude relative to the 14,964 slack.

description: Charge optimistic w4 fixed-matrix denominator update

asi.hypothesis: If w4 lowword matrices are applied as selected fixed windows rather than per-bit full-pair replay, denominator plumbing might drop below the 14,964 CCX slack opened by the w4 selector oracle.
asi.result: Optimistic fixed-matrix replacement improves over naive full-pair replay but still costs mean 2,293.87 CCX/window, projected 321,142 CCX compute (306,178 over slack) and peak 1,660q before any selector/QROM. This kills fixed-matrix full-pair plumbing for the w4 selector near-miss.
asi.next_action_hint: Only consumed-denominator or ratio-specific state updates remain plausible for the w4 BY selector; avoid old+new pair replacement even with free selected matrices.

description: Kill full-ratio BY selector update by A-inverse proxy

asi.hypothesis: A 560-bit full 2-adic ratio state fits the scratch600 history budget, but its A-branch update may require a dense variable inverse that erases the selector win.
asi.result: The serial A-inverse proxy on real 560-step BY traces is mean 7,307,490 CCX and max 8,892,999 before replay body, projecting 9,952,686 Toffoli (gap 7,252,686). This kills full-ratio h-state selector plumbing as a low-scratch SOTA route.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue full-ratio h updates unless a new sparse formula eliminates the per-A inverse; remaining selector routes must be consumed/window-local rather than ratio-only.

description: Quantify consumed-denominator poststate ambiguity at w4

asi.hypothesis: A consumed denominator schedule might avoid explicit full-pair plumbing if post-window denominator state identifies the branch pattern; quantify the ambiguity for w4/w8/w16 reverse windows.
asi.result: Even a 4-step consumed window has 48 valid predecessor branch patterns (6 bits) from the same poststate; depth8 has 1,280 (11 bits), and depth16 has 589,824 (20 bits/states). Branchless poststate recovery is not local, so consumed updates still need explicit pattern/rank history or a reversible window inverse.
asi.next_action_hint: For BY, only designs that consume pattern+q inside a locally reversible window remain; do not assume denominator poststate can erase selector history.

description: Probe plusminus unary parser Google scratch but stale assert assumed failure

asi.hypothesis: The plus-minus unary/self-delimiting parser might exceed the Google 663 scratch allowance in the tail even though p99 was 640 under the strict 600 target.
asi.result: Surprise: on 8192 deterministic samples, unary_scratch_max=657 and entropy_scratch_max=657.125, with over_google_frac=0. The test crashed only because the stale assertion expected max>663. This reopens plus-minus under the 663 Google-low-qubit allowance, but parser Toffoli/cleanup and worst-case exactness remain uncharged.
asi.rollback_reason: Targeted test assertion encoded the old strict-600 conclusion and failed when the Google-663 stress result was actually promising.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the metrics with a non-crashing assertion, then charge plus-minus unary parser cost/cleanup and max/worst-case more honestly before promoting.

description: Reopen plusminus unary parser under Google-663 scratch samples

asi.hypothesis: The plus-minus k-sequence unary/self-delimiting parser was dismissed under a strict 600-scratch filter, but the actual Google allowance is 663; sample max may fit and reopen the route if parser Toffoli/cleanup can be charged.
asi.result: On the existing 8192 deterministic sample, unary scratch p99=640 and max=657, entropy scratch max=657.125, with zero samples over the 663 Google-low-qubit allowance. Boundary-bit parser remains too large (p99=759, max=774). This reopens plus-minus only under the 663 allowance, not strict 600, and only as a sampled state-size result; parser circuit, exact worst-case, rank/dense cleanup, and live-recompute blockers remain.
asi.next_action_hint: Stress plus-minus unary parser with more samples and then charge reversible unary consumption/cleanup. Need exact worst-case or a bounded failure policy before promotion.

description: Open plusminus unary Google663 replay proxy

asi.hypothesis: If the plus-minus unary k stream fits the 663 Google-low-qubit allowance, a scan-style parser might replay one odd-GCD DIV with roughly one add/sub per step and one shift per unary bit, making it worth a real reversible parser experiment.
asi.result: Optimistic floor is strongly SOTA-shaped on 8192 samples: scratch p99/p999/max=639/644/651, no samples over 663; one-DIV proxy p99=286,110 CCX, two-DIV p99=572,220, projected p99 point-add=1,214,936 (gap -1,485,064). This is deliberately not charged for direction sidecar, exact parser circuit, cleanup, worst-case proof, or phase; it only reopens plus-minus unary under the 663 allowance.
asi.next_action_hint: Build an isolated reversible unary-scan parser skeleton for plus-minus steps, including direction handling and cleanup. The first kill criteria are scratch >663, dense direction/rank cleanup, or parser cost exceeding roughly 1.5M slack.

description: Charge plusminus unary parser with existing controlled primitives

asi.hypothesis: The plus-minus unary state/proxy win may disappear once unary bits are charged as quantum controls for modular adds and shifts using existing primitives.
asi.result: Existing controlled add/double/halve primitives each cost 1,280 CCX. With the unary scan counts, one-DIV p99 becomes 1,433,600 CCX, two-DIV p99 2,867,200, projected point-add 3,509,916 (gap +809,916). Scratch stays good (max 650). This kills plus-minus unary with current controlled primitives, but leaves a clear target: controlled parser operations must average far below 1,280 CCX, roughly below ~915 CCX for p99 SOTA with this model.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for Solinas/signed-redundant controlled shift/add fusion for plus-minus unary parser; if no sub-900 CCX controlled step exists, demote plus-minus again.
